title: Scoped notification arriving before any main session falls through to the main pipeline
description: Discovered during cyril-fh06 (PR #54): app.rs routing diverts scoped frames only when session.id().is_some() (app.rs:291 at time of finding) — a RoutedNotification::scoped frame arriving before the main session exists is applied to the main SessionController/UiState instead of being dropped or buffered. Shared by both scoped producers (ToolCallChunk and MetadataUpdated). Not reachable in practice today (both frame kinds follow session creation), so P4/backlog — but it is a latent ordering assumption; decide drop vs buffer and fence it.
acceptance_criteria: A scoped notification delivered before SessionCreated does not mutate main-session state; decision (drop vs buffer) documented in the routing code; regression test covers the pre-session scoped frame.

title: KAS-5b follow-up: reap/kill a session's live terminals on cancel/turn-end (orphan-on-cancel)
description: KAS-5b (cyril-ufie) spawns child processes on the bridge for terminal/create and reaps them on terminal/{release,kill} (the normal KAS flow: create->wait_for_exit->output->release). But if a turn is CANCELLED mid-command, KAS may not send release -> the child keeps running until it exits naturally (e.g. a 60s sleep = a 60s orphan on the bridge process). Scope: on session/cancel (and/or turn-end) kill that session's still-running terminals, keyed by session_id in the terminal registry. Needs the registry to track session_id per terminal (it carries sessionId on every request). Decide policy: kill-on-cancel vs let-finish. Out of KAS-5b's first cut (the happy-path lifecycle); this is the leak-on-cancel edge.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) TRIGGER SITE unnamed in the issue — mechanism (TerminalRegistry, owned by KiroClient at client.rs:26) and trigger (run_loop's Cancel arm bridge.rs:582-598 + TurnCompleted observation) sit on opposite sides of exactly the seam cyril-g9vt deferred. Decide JOINTLY with cyril-g9vt whether this is the ADR-0004 seam's first consumer. Minimal, proportionate option for a P3 leak fix: share Rc<TerminalRegistry> between KiroClient and the loop (same LocalSet thread; registry stays sole owner of process lifecycle) and call reap_session(session_id) from the Cancel arm. A third ad-hoc wiring (e.g. App sending a new reap BridgeCommand) would erode both this and g9vt.
(2) 'Keyed by session_id' should mean a session_id FIELD on Entry (from CreateTerminalRequest.session_id) + linear-scan reap_session — terminal id stays the primary key; a secondary index is over-engineering for a handful of terminals. Reap must follow the existing take-then-await pattern (no RefCell borrow across .await, terminal_io.rs:238-249).
(3) Policy recommendation: apply the existing KILL semantics (id stays valid, partial output cached), NOT release — KAS may still send terminal/output/release for those ids after the cancel, and kill-semantics lets them resolve instead of erroring -32602. Cancel-only for the first cut; reap-at-turn-end can race an in-flight release.
(4) INTERACTION with cyril-r3t6 (see its note): a cancelled turn whose command already wrote >64KB is wedged on the full OS pipe and never exits naturally — kill-on-cancel must also cover pre-wait children, and .kill_on_drop(true) at create is a cheap backstop.

Closed: Fixed: PR #48 (5f97307). Cancelled sessions' running terminals are reaped from run_loop's CancelRequest arm via shared Rc<TerminalRegistry> (session_id on Entry, linear-scan reap_session, KILL semantics — ids stay valid, in-flight children killed through their owning wait). Cancel-only per the issue's first-cut policy. Repro fences: cancel_reaps_sessions_running_terminals (end-to-end, alive-asserted pre-cancel), reap_session_kills_only_that_sessions_children_and_keeps_ids, reap_session_kills_child_owned_by_pending_wait.

title: KAS wrapper mode: extend the C14a login gate (or record why free-path-only) — unservable store currently fails at first callback, not at spawn
description: Flagged during the PR-39 simplifier pass. Both KAS spawn modes run --auth=acp-callback and depend on cyril's getAccessToken responder, but the C14a spawn gate (store_unservable_reason) runs only on the free path (discovery.rs resolve_kas_command); wrapper mode (bridge.rs resolve_spawn_command -> build_wrapper_command) is ungated, so an absent/stale/corrupt store fails as a dead first turn — warn-logged since PR-39 (F1) but not fail-at-spawn. dcc6's design framed C14 free-path-only, possibly deliberately (kiro-cli-the-parent maintains its own login state and may refresh the store; wrapper stale behavior was not probed). Resolution: either extend the gate to wrapper spawns (probe first: does a wrapper spawn with an unservable store fail the same way live?) or record the free-path-only rationale in the code + design doc. Related: cyril-taba (stale affordance), cyril-l7tw (turn-failure visibility).

title: KAS-8 — MCP management subsystem under KAS (_kiro/mcp/*)
description:
KAS-8 functional gap. `_kiro/mcp/*` — the MCP-management subsystem under KAS. v2 exposes MCP via the `/mcp` slash command; KAS exposes it as this `_kiro/*` dialect, which cyril has no handling for.

Surface (covenant):
- Notifications (agent->client): `_kiro/mcp/status`, `_kiro/mcp/governance_disabled`.
- Requests (client->agent): `_kiro/mcp/resetServer {serverName, startOAuth?}`, `_kiro/mcp/getPrompt {serverName, promptName, arguments?}`, `_kiro/mcp/getResource {serverName, uri}`.
- Host callback (agent->client request, gated by advertising): `_kiro/mcp/elicitation {sessionId, toolCallId, elicitation}` -> ElicitationResolveResponse.

SCOPE:
- Consume `_kiro/mcp/status` / `governance_disabled` for an MCP panel (server list + state), the KAS analog of v2 `/mcp`.
- Wire `resetServer` (incl. the OAuth-reset flow — relevant to the 2.8.1 MCP-OAuth clipboard fix) and optionally getPrompt/getResource.
- Decide whether to advertise `mcp/elicitation` (pairs with the userInput / richer-question overlay from CN1).

CAVEAT: this user runs 0 MCP servers, so live capture needs an MCP server configured first.

REFS: covenant §1b/§1c (mcp methods); ROADMAP KAS-8 coverage matrix + KAS-4 (/code,/usage panels are the sibling pattern); memory reference_kiro_2_8_1_diff (MCP OAuth clipboard fix).

title: K1c — Queue-steering polish + parity (optional)
description: Optional; evaluate after K1b. Queue-mode parity with Kiro's Ctrl+S toggle: a client-side buffer that flushes to session/prompt on TurnCompleted instead of steering mid-turn (pure cyril-side, no new wire). Subagent steering: probe _session/steer against subagent session ids, then wire /steer @<name> <msg> through SubagentTracker::find_by_name if it works.
notes: Reconstructed 2026-06-17.

title: @-file completion is silently empty outside a git repo (git ls-files only, no UI feedback)
description: FileCompleter::load shells out to git ls-files and returns an empty completer on failure with only a tracing::warn. In a non-git cwd (or for untracked files) typing @query silently produces no suggestions and the user gets no indication why. Discovered by the cyril-a14l pty oracle: the probe scratch dir was not a git repo and the completer came up empty. Options: fall back to a bounded filesystem walk, or surface a one-time system message ('@-completion unavailable: not a git repository').

title: Windows clippy: cfg-gate transport test Duration import
description:
## What to build

Restore the documented workspace Clippy gate on Windows. The transport test module imports `std::time::Duration` unconditionally, but every consumer is `cfg(unix)`, so Windows all-targets Clippy rejects the unused import before unrelated feature slices can pass their gate.

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` no longer reports the transport `Duration` import on Windows.
- [ ] Unix transport timeout tests retain access to `Duration` and continue to compile.
- [ ] The full workspace quality gate passes.
notes: Closed: Cfg-gated the Unix-only Duration import; Windows cyril-core all-targets Clippy and tests pass.

title: Watch: acp rpc parse ERROR on oversized/split inbound frame (F4 baseline observation)
description: Observed during the cyril-6iek F4 baseline (.cyril-6iek/test_bridge-default-vs-kas.out line 71, kiro-cli 2.12.0 KAS wrapper under a default-build v2 binding): agent_client_protocol::rpc logged 'failed to parse incoming message: expected value at line 1 column 1. Raw: ry...' — the Raw content is the TAIL of a large commands/tools frame, i.e. one wire line appears to have been split across reads and the fragment failed to parse. The mismatched-engine configuration that produced it is now fail-stopped at initialize by cyril-6iek, but the underlying question stands: can a LEGITIMATE (--features kas) session hit the same split on a very large frame (huge MCP tool list)? KAS-2a live runs and the 2.11.0 traces handled full command lists fine, so evidence is thin — watch item, not a repro. Probe idea: feed a >64KiB single-line frame through the acp 0.10.2 rpc read path (in-process duplex) and see whether it splits. Related: cyril-1ixa (rpc-layer buffering), cyril-p7kp (acp deserialization hard-fail watch).

title: KAS inlineAgents: probe + expose per-subagent model/effort spawning (dormant _meta.kiro.settings flag)
description:
Discovery (2026-07-17, 2.13.0 KAS bundle static analysis): KAS's invoke_sub_agent tool has a dormant 'inlineAgent' parameter — INLINE_AGENT_SCHEMA {systemPrompt, model?, effort?} — letting the supervising LLM spawn an ephemeral one-off agent with a per-spawn model and reasoning-effort level ('second-opinion reviewer on a different model — without authoring an agent file'). Effort is clamped to the chosen model's catalog-advertised effortLevels; unsupported/omitted falls back one tier below max (belowMax); inline agents inherit parent tools/permissions, never widened. Gated by the 'inlineAgents' AgentSettings key (isSettingEnabled(settings,'inlineAgents'), acp-workspace-connection.ts), default OFF ('Enabled for evals first; flip the default later'). Present + dormant since >=2.11.0 (KAS 0.8.0) with the effort field from the start — release audits never flagged it because the flag never flipped. Sibling gate: 'subagentOrchestration' enables the OrchestrateSubAgent DAG tool the same way.

Why cyril: no on-disk agent config on EITHER engine can express per-agent effort (v2 Agent struct: deny_unknown_fields, no effort field; KAS agent files: no effort field, z.object silently strips strays). This flag is the only per-subagent effort mechanism in Kiro, and it is wire-drivable: cyril can flip it via session/new _meta.kiro.settings on the KAS engine — a capability Kiro's own frontends don't enable yet.

Plan: (1) live A/B probe once auth is available — session/new with settings.inlineAgents enabled vs control; confirm invoke_sub_agent schema gains inlineAgent (visible in tool listing / covenant), have supervisor spawn low-effort + xhigh-effort inline agents, verify effort lands (metadata/effortLevel on subtask, thinking-chunk presence as proxy) and the belowMax clamp. Per feedback_kiro_schema_vs_runtime: schema-accepted != functional — the flag may be additionally server-gated for evals. (2) If functional, decide cyril exposure: enable-by-default on KAS sessions vs /settings toggle; document in KAS wire audit. Full static evidence recorded in memory reference_kiro_effort_schema_negotiation (KAS inline-agent section).
acceptance_criteria: Live A/B capture proving whether settings.inlineAgents=on changes the invoke_sub_agent schema and whether inline effort levels take effect on the wire (or documented negative result); decision recorded on whether/how cyril enables the flag; KAS wire-audit doc updated.

title: Bridge: engine death and prompt failure are invisible — prompt Err collapses to TurnCompleted(EndTurn); io-task death log-only; fail-stop BridgeDisconnected droppable
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review. Engine-agnostic bridge code, but KAS makes it material: the most likely real-world KAS failure (the node process crashing or wedging mid-turn) currently presents as an agent that quietly stopped answering.

(1) The off-loop prompt task maps conn.prompt() Err to StopReason::EndTurn and emits ONLY TurnCompleted — no BridgeError (bridge.rs:563-577). Directly violates CLAUDE.md 'Bridge errors must notify the App — logging alone is invisible.'
(2) The io pump's death is tracing::error! only (bridge.rs:395-399). The acp crate clears pending_responses when io ends (rpc.rs:78), so a mid-turn crash resolves the prompt as Err -> the user sees the turn just end with zero output and zero explanation. The loop stays alive against the dead connection: every subsequent SendPrompt errors fast and again produces a silent instant TurnCompleted — the session looks idle-and-ignoring-me until the user happens to run /new (whose arm DOES emit BridgeDisconnected).
(3) This also swallows the auth responder's actionable errors: auth.rs:160-163 returns 'kiro token expired; run kiro-cli login' TO KAS, not to the App — KAS fails the turn and the user gets silence.
(4) Sub-item: the fail-stop BridgeDisconnected uses try_send (bridge.rs:163-174) and is dropped with only a warn! if the notification channel is full — plausible exactly during a heavy streaming backlog, the moment a crash is most likely. Use a bounded send with timeout or a reserved slot.

NOTE: the turn_in_flight gate itself is safe (the Err arm still synthesizes TurnCompleted, so the gate always clears) — the problem is purely that the failure is mute.
acceptance_criteria: Killing the agent process mid-turn surfaces a visible error to the user (BridgeError and/or BridgeDisconnected) in the same turn; a SendPrompt against a dead connection produces an actionable error message, not a silent TurnCompleted; the fail-stop disconnect signal cannot be dropped by a full channel; auth-stale turn failures reach the user with the actionable login hint.
notes:
EVIDENCE (2026-07-04, dcc6 C14b): the collapse is live-confirmed dangerous — the un-hardened KAS smokes green-lit on TurnCompleted alone, which would also pass on an auth-failed turn. Interim mitigation: kas_freepath_smoke + kas_wrapper_smoke now assert an echoed KAS_SMOKE_OK sentinel. The real fix (prompt Err surfacing as its own notification, not TurnCompleted(EndTurn)) remains this issue's scope.

PR-39 REVIEW MITIGATION (2026-07-04): item (3)'s invisibility is now half-addressed — every getAccessToken failure path warn-logs locally (cyril.log breadcrumb) before the JSON-RPC error travels to KAS (get_access_token_from, auth.rs; review finding F1). The App-notification half (surfacing to the UI, plus the general prompt-Err collapse) remains this issue's scope; the responder is a free function with no channel access, so the fix belongs with this issue's bridge rework.

STDERR-TAIL HANDOFF (2026-07-04, cyril-0gke PR #41): AgentProcess now drains stderr and exposes stderr_tail() (last-50-line StderrTail ring buffer); the IO-pump failure log includes the snapshot. Remaining for THIS issue: thread the tail into user-facing BridgeDisconnected reasons — the bridge-exit BridgeDisconnected emission (bridge.rs ~:167) lives OUTSIDE run_bridge, so the tail handle must be passed up to that layer.

Closed: Shipped: PR #45 merged to main (252a620). All 4 sub-items + ACs fenced: (1) prompt Err emits BridgeError before TurnCompleted (prompt_error_emits_bridge_error_before_completion, death_mid_turn_* fences); (2) io-pump watcher — probe found SIGKILL is a CLEAN EOF so the watcher keys on pump END not Err; idle death disconnects+exits, mid-turn death defers disconnect until the turn's terminal marker (BridgeError->TurnCompleted->BridgeDisconnected); (3) KAS auth-callback Err emits BridgeError(auth) with LOGIN_HINT; v2 login hint reaches the user via stderr tail in disconnect reasons (live-verified: logged-out kiro-cli 2.11.0 run 4 shows 'not logged in' in the reason); (4) fail-stop disconnect survives a full channel via 5s-bounded send (mutation-verified fence). Build caught+fixed a real drain-dedup bug (KAS dual completion could reach the App twice). Fixed in-branch: scope-creep commit rebuilt, named C6 fence restored. Filed: cyril-gua0 (respawn/reconnect). Audit trail: .cyril-l7tw/ (probe runs 1-4, design 13 claims, plan 8 slices).

title: KAS-1 — Engine selection + _kiro/auth/getAccessToken responder (the entry gate)
description:
The precondition for every KAS milestone below. Without a host-supplied token, every KAS turn dies immediately ([TokenInvalidError] ... Host refresh callback returned no access token).

SCOPE (ROADMAP KAS-1, docs/ROADMAP.md:154):
- Make the KAS spawn real: the AgentEngine gate from KAS-0 (cyril-atjw) resolves the KAS engine to a live process; default stays v2. All new KAS code lands behind the 'kas' cargo feature from KAS-0.
  NOTE / CORRECTION: the engine flag was renamed 'kas' -> 'v3' as of kiro-cli 2.8.0 (probe-verified: 'kas' is rejected, possible values are v1/v2/v3; spawn is 'kiro-cli acp --agent-engine v3'). 2.7.1 accepted 'kas'. Resolve the engine string per installed version, don't hardcode 'kas'.
- Implement a '_kiro/auth/getAccessToken' server->client request responder: reply {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn}. KAS validates expiresAt > now + ~3 min and REQUIRES profileArn (backend 400s 'profileArn is required' without it).
- Token sourcing — MIRROR Kiro's own responder, don't just read the sqlite row. Kiro (crates/chat-cli-v2/src/auth/{kas_token,refresh_coordinator,social,builder_id,external_idp}.rs): (1) resolves the active token across THREE types — social (GitHub -> kirocli:social:token, carries profile_arn), AWS Builder ID, external IdP (kirocli:external-idp:token); (2) proactively OIDC-refreshes before the ~3-min pre-expiry buffer via a lock-guarded refresh_coordinator; (3) answers {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn}. A naive 'read kirocli:social:token and return it' responder breaks on the refresh buffer (the failure our probes hit) and on Builder-ID/external-IdP users. Cleanest: DELEGATE to kiro-cli's own auth rather than reimplement OIDC refresh + multi-type resolution.
- This makes cyril a CUSTODIAN of a kiro credential — handle as such (no logging, read-only, minimal lifetime). See ROADMAP Open Tension #7.

This is the AUTH half of the initialize._meta.kiro (KiroClientMeta) handshake; the SETTINGS half is cyril-nhzw (AgentSettings).

REFS: ROADMAP KAS-1 (docs/ROADMAP.md:154); covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md (KiroClientMeta handshake); memory reference_kiro_kas_launch_contract.
acceptance_criteria:
* Engine-select plumbing resolves the KAS engine to a live spawn (version-correct flag: v3 on >=2.8.0, kas on 2.7.1); default remains v2.
* '_kiro/auth/getAccessToken' responder replies {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn} and is exercised in a gated end-to-end smoke against a live KAS session.
* Auth-responder unit test covers the reply shape AND expired-token rejection (expiresAt <= now + ~3 min).
* profileArn is always present in the reply (Builder-ID / external-IdP users included), sourced by delegating to kiro-cli's own auth.
* Credential is never logged; token read is read-only and minimal-lifetime.
notes:
2026-06-21 — FREE PATH CONFIRMED; de-risks the entry gate (auth responder is no longer a hard prerequisite for a first live KAS turn).

Verified live: kiro-cli 2.8.1, @kiro/agent 0.3.257, experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-direct-spawn-2.8.1.py. KAS's selectAuthProvider resolves a --auth=user|machine|acp-callback flag (default user). The two spawn shapes differ:
  - WRAPPER spawn `kiro-cli acp --agent-engine v3` injects --auth=acp-callback -> KAS calls _kiro/auth/getAccessToken back on cyril -> the responder (this issue's main scope) is MANDATORY. This is the [TokenInvalidError] failure our earlier probes hit.
  - DIRECT spawn `node --experimental-wasm-modules <.../@kiro/agent/dist/server/acp-server.js> --transport=stdio` with NO --auth flag -> default FileAuthProvider reads ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json and self-refreshes via the file's refreshToken. _kiro/auth/getAccessToken fired 0 times across a full initialize + session/new + tool-using turn (stopReason end_turn). stderr logged "[INFO] Auth: default token file".

IMPLICATION: a first live KAS turn (cyril-j16p / KAS-2a) is reachable with ZERO credential-custodian code as long as the user has run `kiro-cli login`. Open Tension #7 moves OFF the walking-skeleton critical path.

SUGGESTED SPLIT (for triage, not yet applied):
  - tier-1 = free-path direct spawn, no responder. Unblocks the demo.
  - tier-2 = the _kiro/auth/getAccessToken acp-callback responder for the blessed wrapper lifecycle (kiro-cli token-sync + lock-guarded refresh-coordinator) and Builder-ID / external-IdP users.

Direct-spawn trade-off: cyril must locate the server entry (KIRO_KAS_SERVER_PATH env, else walk node_modules/@kiro/agent/dist/server/acp-server.js), the node runtime (KIRO_AGENT_PATH), and pass --experimental-wasm-modules — all of which the wrapper handles for free.

ROADMAP KAS-1 updated with this. Full --auth/--transport/sandbox flag contract + 5-tier auth priority in memory reference_kiro_kas_launch_contract.

2026-06-21 (fileMatch-steering probe session) — FOURTH token type for the tier-2 acp-callback responder, NOT covered by the three above: an IAM Identity Center / OIDC-PKCE login stores under auth_kv key `kirocli:odic:token` = {access_token, expires_at, refresh_token, region, start_url, oauth_flow, scopes} and carries NO profile_arn. For that case the matching profileArn lives in a SEPARATE row: auth_kv->`state` table, key `api.codewhisperer.profile`. Proven pitfall: pairing an OIDC accessToken with a DIFFERENT identity's profileArn (e.g. a stale ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token*.json, different account/region) -> backend 'Access denied'; accessToken and profileArn MUST be the same identity. Reinforces 'don't naively read one sqlite row': the responder must resolve profileArn per token type (social/Builder-ID embed it; odic splits it into state/api.codewhisperer.profile). Working assembly verified end-to-end in experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-filematch-steering-v2.py (odic access_token + state profileArn -> live KAS turn succeeded). Caveat for the direct-spawn 'free path': it reads ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json, but those files can be STALE/expired (they were expired this session for an odic-active user), so the free path depends on that file being kept fresh. See memory reference_kiro_kas_filematch_steering_source.

title: KAS context bar can overflow the status line on narrow terminals (clips credits + scroll hint)
description:
Introduced by KAS-2b (cyril-5et2, PR #33). render_status_bar (crates/cyril-ui/src/widgets/toolbar.rs:136) appends the 5-label KAS context breakdown bar ('Context Files X%  Session Files Y%  Tools Z%  Prompts P%  Responses R%', ~70 cols) to the same single, non-wrapping Line that already carries 'Context: N%', credits, tokens, stop-reason, and the browse-mode 'SCROLL ↓ PgDn' hint. The Paragraph renders without wrap, so on a narrow terminal (e.g. 80 cols) everything past the right edge is clipped.

REPRO: run a KAS session in an ~80-col terminal; once a context_usage frame with a breakdown arrives, both 'Context: N%' and the 5-label bar render. The breakdown bar is pushed BEFORE credits and the scroll hint, so those later segments fall off-screen — hiding the affordance that tells the user how to leave browse/follow mode.

Pre-existing single-line truncation mechanism, but the wide unconditional bar newly makes it reachable. Graceful (no panic) — purely a layout/UX regression.

FIX is a design choice, not a one-liner: e.g. a compact/responsive form when width is constrained, eliding the redundant scalar when the breakdown is present, abbreviating labels, or measuring remaining width before appending. Out of scope for the KAS-2b bar itself; surfaced by /code-review max on PR #33.

REFS: toolbar.rs:115-156 (render_status_bar); related cyril-5et2 (KAS-2b).
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #47 (c7d0ed5). render_status_bar measures the full line first and omits the KAS breakdown bar when it would clip trailing segments (credits, stop reason, SCROLL hint); the Context scalar always stays. Repro fences: narrow_status_bar_keeps_scroll_hint_over_breakdown (80 cols), wide_status_bar_keeps_breakdown_bar (200 cols). Gemini perf note (double status_bar_spans call) rejected with evidence: ~15 spans at max 20fps is microseconds; drain-variant couples caller to span indices.

title: Cache completed Write diff derivations
description: Review finding #13: completed Write tool calls recompute the same Myers diff twice per frame, including off-screen history. Derive summary and lines once or use a bounded coherent cache.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] One diff derivation supplies both summary and rendered lines.
- [ ] Historical rendering does not recompute unchanged diffs each frame.
- [ ] Coherence tests and a repeated-render benchmark pass.

title: KAS-8 — Model _meta.kiro.hookConfirm + KAS agent-file hook types; render Stop-hook confirm dialogs
description:
From the 2026-07-17 KAS agent-file schema export (kiro-cli 2.13.0 / @kiro/agent 0.18.2; docs/kiro-kas-agent-*-schema-2.13.0.*, experiments/kas-agent-schema-export/): KAS agent files can embed inline hooks (landed 2.12.3) with a 'confirm' block that is honored on the Stop/agentStop trigger — when the agent tries to end its turn, the user is asked confirm.question with confirm.options[]; run:true options execute the hook command (whose output decides {continue, reason}), run:false options skip it and, with continueReason set, convert the turn-stop into keep-working with that reason fed to the agent (verified in src/hooks/triggers/stop.ts).

Wire shape (rides plain ACP, no new method): synthetic pending tool_call (toolCallId hook-confirm-<uuid>, kind 'other') tagged _meta.kiro.hookConfirm {kind:'stop', hookName, question} -> standard session/request_permission (optionId=option.id, name=label, kind=allow_once if run else reject_once) -> terminal tool_call_update with _meta.kiro.hookConfirm.decision.

Cyril work: (1) model the _meta.kiro.hookConfirm payload in convert/kas.rs (pending + decision variants) per the model-full-wire-surface rule; (2) render hook-confirm approvals as a distinct dialog (hook name + question) instead of a generic tool permission — note cyril filters kind-Other tool_calls from display, so without the _meta tag the pending marker is invisible and the permission prompt shows with no context; (3) verify the permission-response path returns arbitrary optionIds — a confirm block can have MULTIPLE reject_once options distinguished only by optionId/continueReason (cyril-qo13 fixed first-option-only for kind-keyed responses; confirm that fix covers this shape); (4) optionally: type the agent-file HookDocument/HookConfirm/HookAction shapes (committed JSON Schemas are the reference) for any future hooks-panel display of KAS inline hooks — current hooks panel models v2 HookInfo {trigger, command, matcher} only.

Semantics caveats for tests: confirm is Stop-trigger-only (schema allows it anywhere; other triggers ignore it); with no UI attached KAS skips confirm-hooks entirely, so absence of the dialog is not absence of the hook.
acceptance_criteria: convert/kas.rs parses _meta.kiro.hookConfirm (pending question + terminal decision) with unknown-field logging; approval overlay shows hook name + question for hookConfirm requests; regression test proving a 3-option confirm (run/skip/keep-working) can return each optionId; decision recorded on KAS inline-hook display in the hooks panel.
notes: WIRE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 (probe-kas-hook-confirm-2.13.0.py, capture kas-hook-confirm-2.13.0.json, all 6 verdicts PASS): (1) pending tool_call hook-confirm-<uuid> kind=other + _meta.kiro.hookConfirm {kind:stop, hookName, question}; (2) requestPermission options run:true->allow_once, run:false->reject_once; (3) continueReason injected as NEW human message wrapped <HOOK_INSTRUCTION>...</HOOK_INSTRUCTION> + graph restart — agent obeyed it (HELLOTURTLES); (4) terminal tool_call_update carries decision=<optionId> with status=FAILED for reject_once picks — rendering caveat: a successful keep-working choice arrives as a failed tool call; (5) once-per-turn guard onAgentStopHooksExecuted (shared-graph-nodes.ts): stop hooks fire at most ONCE per turn, continuation runs unguarded to end_turn; (6) handshake gate = clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.hooks {enabled:true, v2:true} — v2 activates the standalone .kiro/hooks/*.json loader; fresh-git-dir ACP session counted workspaceTrusted.

title: Activate bundled themes and terminal color modes
description:
## What to build

Expose the already-defined bundled themes and terminal color modes through startup configuration and a session-local `/theme` picker. The picker previews highlighted themes immediately, accepts with Enter, and restores the previous session theme on Esc without rewriting configuration. This ticket activates selection; it does not design additional palettes.

## Blocked by

- cyril-cc5e
- cyril-ghuu
- cyril-nrnq
- cyril-dij8
- cyril-fkke
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Startup configuration selects any installed bundled theme and supports automatic, true-color, ANSI-256, ANSI-16, and no-color modes.
- [ ] Automatic mode respects NO_COLOR unless the operator explicitly overrides the color mode.
- [ ] The `/theme` picker previews immediately, Enter accepts, and Esc restores the prior session theme without rewriting configuration.
- [ ] Invalid configuration follows the pinned fallback behavior and the full workspace quality gate passes.

title: Render v2 model-refusal alerts: parse refusal object on kiro.dev/metadata (2.12.1)
description:
kiro-cli 2.12.1 adds Model Refusal Alerts: when the backend refuses (content policy / content filter), the v2 engine emits an optional refusal object on the kiro.dev/metadata notification — {category, explanation, recommendedModel} (camelCase on wire; confirmed from carved 2.12.1 tui.js handler: broadcastStreamEvent({type:'model_refusal', stopReason, category:r?.category, explanation:r?.explanation, recommendedModel:r?.recommendedModel})) — and metadata stopReason can be 'CONTENT_FILTERED'. The session/prompt response can also return ACP stop_reason: refusal.

Cyril today: to_stop_reason maps Refusal correctly and toolbar shows 'Refused' (red), but convert/kiro.rs kiro.dev/metadata parsing (to_ext_notification) reads only contextUsagePercentage/meteringUsage/tokens/effort — the refusal object is silently dropped. Kiro's own TUI shows a transient error alert plus a system chat message with the explanation (fallback text suggests switching model / new conversation / rewind). Cyril users get a bare 'Refused' with no why and no recommended model.

Scope: extend the MetadataUpdated notification (or add a dedicated notification) with Option<RefusalInfo {category, explanation, recommended_model}>, surface it in UiState as a system message + toolbar state. Guard partial updates per design principles (all three fields optional). Note: runtime emission not yet live-verified (cannot force a backend refusal on demand) — schema evidence is from binary serde strings + tui.js handler; per feedback_kiro_schema_vs_runtime treat wire keys as provisional until first live refusal capture. Stale doc comment types/session.rs:497-499 ('bridge currently hardcodes EndTurn') should be deleted in passing — bridge.rs:794 extracts the real stop reason.

2.12.3 audit addendum (docs/kiro-2.12.3-wire-audit.md): refusal handling grew in tui.js (8->13 tokens); 'refusal' is now a FIRST-CLASS stopReason zod literal with two emit paths -> model_refusal {stopReason, category, explanation, recommendedModel} (KAS/_kiro side). When implementing, tolerate stopReason 'refusal' in addition to metadata stopReason 'CONTENT_FILTERED'. Still no live refusal capture; keys remain provisional.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A kiro.dev/metadata payload carrying refusal {category, explanation, recommendedModel} produces a user-visible explanation in the chat (system message) and does not disturb existing metadata fields; 2) absent refusal field = no behavior change; 3) refusal with missing/partial subfields renders whatever is present without defaults or panics; 4) stale TurnSummary doc comment removed; 5) state + render tests per testing-layers convention.

title: acp rpc layer buffers notifications unboundedly under UI stall (no agent-side backpressure)
description: Tracking note from the 2026-07-01 issue-set reliability review — filed so the failure signature is searchable; no action until observed. The vendored agent-client-protocol read loop pushes every notification through incoming_tx.unbounded_send (rpc.rs handle_io) and spawns each handler as its own local task, which then awaits cyril's BOUNDED inbound channel (cap 256, bridge.rs:16/374). Verified during review: no deadlock is possible at the rpc layer (responses resolve inline; the 32-slot command channel circular wait is not realistically reachable) — but no backpressure ever reaches the agent process either. If the App stalls (worst realistic case: tty flow-control-stopped via Ctrl+S while terminal.draw blocks, app.rs:228+) during a heavy KAS streaming turn, the unbounded queue plus parked handler tasks grow without bound for the duration of the turn. Memory growth bounded by turn size, not a deadlock. Related: cyril-9akh lives in the same rpc.rs notification-vs-response plumbing.

Keep the covenant's read-covenant-FIRST role (CLAUDE.md) intact — this is a re-sync, not a restructure. Related: cyril-45ld (0.8.0 new-surface watch), cyril-jd8e (v2 doc counterpart).

title: SessionController.context_usage not reset on SessionCreated/BridgeDisconnected (diverges from UiState)
description:
Latent divergence surfaced during the cyril-5et2 (KAS-2b) code review (PR #33), deliberately left unfixed to avoid scope creep into a file the PR didn't touch.

PR #33 added a SessionController arm for Notification::ContextBreakdownUpdated (crates/cyril-core/src/session.rs) so the documented 'context usage flows to both state machines' invariant (the context_usage_flows_to_both routing test) holds under KAS, where the scalar arrives only via that notification. UiState was also updated to RESET context_usage + context_breakdown on SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected (so a prior session's 'Context: N%'/bars don't leak across /new or linger on a dead connection).

SessionController's SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected arms do NOT clear self.context_usage, so after /new or disconnect SessionController retains the prior session's value while UiState clears it — the two state machines the App feeds (app.rs:307-308) now disagree on context lifecycle.

UNOBSERVABLE TODAY: nothing in non-test code reads SessionController::context_usage() (only the toolbar shows context, and it reads UiState). This is a trap for the next CommandContext consumer that reads session.context_usage() (e.g. a future /context- or /stats-style command), which would see stale-after-/new data. Note the gap is engine-agnostic — SessionController never reset context_usage on /new even on the v2 MetadataUpdated path; KAS-2b made it newly relevant because SessionController now tracks the KAS scalar too.

FIX (trivial, ~1 line each): add self.context_usage = None; to SessionController's SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected arms, mirroring the UiState fix. No observable behavior change today.

PRECEDENT: cyril-85py tracks the analogous unread/divergent SessionController.steering_depth vs the optimistic UiState chip.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #46 (6918f99). SessionController now resets context_usage on SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected, mirroring UiState. Repro fences: context_usage_resets_on_session_created, context_usage_resets_on_disconnect (the latter ingests via ContextBreakdownUpdated so the KAS path is fenced).

description: Verified 2026-07-05: the doc is v2-only by construction (baseline = kiro-tui-2.0.1.js; KAS mentioned only as 'scaffolding, not yet on the wire' in s11.7 — predates the 2.7.1 KAS landing) and last verified at 2.4.1. title: dispatch_code_command Prompt path drops the message when busy (second cyril-2vcc path)
description: Code-review (cyril-bm1j, xhigh) found a SECOND instance of the cyril-2vcc bug class, outside the K1b diff so deferred. crates/cyril/src/app.rs dispatch_code_command's CodeCommandResponse::Prompt arm (app.rs:1152-1171) add_user_message()s + set_activity(Sending) + emits BridgeCommand::SendPrompt with NO busy guard. If a /code response yields a Prompt while session.status()==Busy, the post-cyril-84ca one-turn guard rejects the second SendPrompt with BridgeError -> the injected prompt is committed to chat but never sent, and activity regresses to Sending. cyril-2vcc fixed only submit_input (Enter-while-busy) via classify_submit->dispatch_steer; this /code prompt-injection path was not covered. FIX: gate this path on busy too (advisory or defer), mirroring the submit_input fix. Reachable when a user triggers a /code prompt-injection mid-turn.
design:
Falsifiable design (gilfoyle): .cyril-8ej2/falsifiable-design.md. Cheapest falsifier RUN + PASSED (busy+Prompt currently emits SendPrompt+UserText+Sending — bug confirmed).

DECISION: busy-case = advisory + drop (zero bridge commands, commit nothing, one System advisory), NOT steer — because dispatch_code_command is sync (steer path is async) and the issue specifies advisory/defer. Scope = Busy only, matching classify_submit.

7 claims, each with an independent unit-test falsifier + named buggy impl (non-vacuity) + a distinct regression fence:
1 Active+Prompt unchanged (1 SendPrompt, UserText, Sending) — extend dispatch_code_prompt_returns_deferred_command
2 Busy+Prompt emits zero commands — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_drops_no_send
3 Busy+Prompt commits no UserText — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_commits_no_user_message
4 Busy+Prompt doesn't set Sending — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_leaves_activity
5 Busy+Prompt adds System advisory — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_advises
6 no-session+Prompt unchanged — dispatch_code_prompt_no_session_shows_error (existing)
7 guard scoped to Prompt arm: Panel opens when Busy — dispatch_code_panel_opens_when_busy

Additive change (adds a guard; removes no invariant). Negative space: doesn't steer, doesn't guard Compacting/Initializing/Disconnected, doesn't queue/retry, doesn't touch Panel/Unknown/success-false, doesn't go async.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED on branch fix/cyril-8ej2-code-prompt-busy-guard, commit 1f44a2d. All gilfoyle gates green.
- Fix: runtime busy-guard in dispatch_code_command Prompt arm (crates/cyril/src/app.rs) — Busy => advise + drop (0 commands, no UserText, activity untouched). Scope = Busy only (matches classify_submit). Runtime check, not debug_assert (load-bearing for correctness in release).
- Fences (7, all pass): dispatch_code_prompt_{busy_drops_no_send, busy_commits_no_user_message, busy_leaves_activity, busy_advises}, dispatch_code_panel_opens_when_busy, dispatch_code_prompt_returns_deferred_command (extended: asserts UserText), dispatch_code_prompt_no_session_shows_error.
- RED->GREEN verified (4 busy fences failed pre-guard). Full workspace: 770 tests pass, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt --check clean.
- Artifacts: .cyril-8ej2/{falsifiable-design,budgeted-plan}.md.
Leave open pending review/merge (close per the project's PR-close pattern). NOT pushed.

Closed: Shipped: PR #27 merged to main (e5d397c). Busy-guard in dispatch_code_command Prompt arm: Busy => advise + drop (0 commands, no UserText, activity untouched). All 7 design fences on main (dispatch_code_prompt_busy_*, panel_opens_when_busy, returns_deferred_command, no_session_shows_error). Stale local branch fix/cyril-8ej2-code-prompt-busy-guard is fully superseded.

title: KAS-5b follow-up: honor terminal/create outputByteLimit (truncate at char boundary)
description: KAS-5b (cyril-ufie) ships terminal/output with truncated:false and the full combined output. acp CreateTerminalRequest carries output_byte_limit: Option<u64> (ACP: when output exceeds the limit, the Client truncates from the BEGINNING to stay within it, at a UTF-8 char boundary, setting truncated:true). NOT production-reachable @ kiro-cli 2.10.0 — the host_callbacks_2.10.0.json capture shows KAS sends no outputByteLimit (None). Implement tail-keep truncation + truncated flag when/if a capture shows KAS sending it. Gate: re-probe a KAS release for outputByteLimit on terminal/create before building.

title: Probe: KAS autonomous mode — the last unexercised mode-matrix cell (sandbox interplay)
description: Every other KAS mode is now live-verified (docs/kiro-kas-modes-2.10.0.md + 2026-07-05 addenda): plan is read-only incl. its subagent roster; mode switching is per-turn _meta.kiro.modeId (session-level setters are cosmetic mid-session); customAgents inject via _meta.kiro on session/new; spec execution (executeTask + runAllTasks) verified end-to-end with sequential spec-task-execution subagents. AUTONOMOUS remains unexercised. Probe questions: (1) what does autonomous change vs vibe — permission suppression (does it finally stop firing request_permission for writes/shell, unlike autopilot=on which did NOT suppress in spec-flow modes)? (2) interplay with _kiro/sandbox/applyConfig (NEW in @kiro/agent 0.8.0 — is a sandbox config required/advisory/enforced before autonomous runs? What shape?), (3) subagent roster + orchestration behavior, (4) turn-loop bounds (agentIterationLimit? runaway risk in a throwaway workspace). Method: same harness as probe-kas-plan-handoff2-2.11.0.py (per-turn modeId), throwaway git workspace, vibe control run, git-porcelain oracle, auto-approve-and-count permissions. CAUTION: autonomous + tools:* in a non-sandboxed cwd — keep the workspace disposable and the prompt narrowly scoped; probe sandbox/applyConfig FIRST. Related: reference_kiro_2_11_0_diff (sandbox/applyConfig arrival), docs/kiro-kas-modes-2.10.0.md ('autonomous still open — needs sandbox design').

title: KAS auth on Windows host: sqlite store lives inside WSL — free-path discovery+responder need a WSL-aware store path
description: cyril-dcc6 moves the KAS auth source to ~/.local/share/kiro-cli/data.sqlite3 (CLI sqlite store) and free-path discovery to ~/.local/share/kiro-cli/kas/<ver>-<sha>/. On Windows, cyril spawns 'wsl kiro-cli acp' and both paths are WSL-internal — a Windows-host cyril cannot read them via native paths (would need \\wsl$ translation via platform/path.rs, and rusqlite over \\wsl$ is untested). dcc6 scopes to Linux/WSL-side execution; this issue tracks the Windows-host story (or an explicit unsupported-with-actionable-error decision).
acceptance_criteria: Windows-host KAS spawn either works (store+bundle resolved through WSL translation) or fails loud with an actionable message; decision recorded in docs.

title: KAS allow_always consent-scope echo: live control probe, then optional consent passthrough on permission responses
description: Split from cyril-qo13 (its widened-scope halves b+c for KAS). The qo13 prove-it probe RESOLVED the allow_once case: the reference client itself omitted the _meta.kiro.consent echo on trace requests 8/9 (present on 6/7, all consentRound=1) and all four fs_writes completed -> consent echo is OPTIONAL for invocation-scope grants; cyril need not send it. UNVERIFIED remainder: allow_always. The reference outcome shape for allow_once was scope:'invocation'; presumably always-accept echoes scope:'session'|'always', and it is unknown whether KAS persists the grant scope WITHOUT that echo (schema-present != required, feedback_kiro_schema_vs_runtime). CONTROL PROBE: live KAS session (python raw JSON-RPC template in experiments/conductor-spike/), trigger fs_write approval, reply optionId:'always-accept' WITH NO consent _meta, then re-trigger the same capability+resource and check whether KAS re-asks (grant not persisted) or proceeds (persisted). If required: add an engine-conditional consent{capability,scope,workspaceRoot} passthrough on the permission response via convert/kas.rs (the KAS seam), echoing request _meta.kiro.consent.capability/workspaceRoot + scope derived from the picked option. Evidence base: experiments/conductor-spike/kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl + .cyril-qo13/probe-findings.md.
notes:
CONSENT SHAPE DRIFTS ACROSS BUNDLES (2026-07-02, KAS modes probe — docs/kiro-kas-modes-2.10.0.md wire delta #4): the request-side consent _meta is not a stable struct. 2.10.0 bundle (@kiro/agent 0.3.299, modes-probe capture): {capability, resource, askType, triggeringResource, workspaceRoot} — consentRound ABSENT, triggeringResource present. 2.11.0 trace (0.8.0): consentRound present (=1 on reqs 6/7), no triggeringResource observed. Implication for the passthrough half of this issue: do NOT model consent as a typed struct with required fields — echo the request's consent object opaquely (serde_json::Value or all-optional fields), adding only the derived scope. A typed consent that requires consentRound or triggeringResource will break on the other bundle line. Raw evidence: experiments/conductor-spike/kas-modes-dumps/bug-fix.json (first permission frame) vs kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl.

LIVE DATAPOINT (2026-07-02, qo13 C8 probe, KAS 2.11.0 direct-spawn): replied {outcome:{outcome:'selected',optionId:'always-accept'}} (NO consent _meta) to the first execute approval; turn proceeded BUT two further permission requests still arrived in the same turn (different resources/commands). Consistent with per-resource/per-capability consent scoping rather than the always grant failing outright — but ambiguous without inspecting the follow-up requests' consent.resource. The control probe this issue plans should compare same-resource re-asks specifically.

title: Add multiline navigation and bounded undo to message input
description:
## What to build

Deepen Cyril's custom message input rather than replacing it. Multiline prompts gain logical-line navigation and a bounded edit history while existing send, newline, paste, and autocomplete behavior remains intact.

## Blocked by

- cyril-ghuu
- cyril-a14l
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Up/Down navigate logical lines while preserving desired column, and Home/End target the current logical line.
- [ ] The latest 100 edit transactions support Ctrl+Z undo and Ctrl+Y or Ctrl+Shift+Z redo.

title: K1a steering non-functional: __session/steer double-underscore + dead inbound converter arm
description: prove-it-prototype (cyril-bm1j) against kiro-cli 2.8.0 found K1a steering broken end-to-end in both directions, missed by K1a unit tests (they bypass the real wire). (a) OUTBOUND: bridge.rs:1060/1127 call ExtRequest::new("_session/steer") but agent-client-protocol does format!("_{}",method) (lib.rs:213) -> wire shows __session/steer -> kiro -32601. Fix: pass "session/steer" / "session/steer/clear" (cf. SpawnSession's "session/spawn"). (b) INBOUND: echoes ride wire _kiro.dev/session/update; the lib strips the leading _ (lib.rs:294) so the converter sees kiro.dev/session/update; K1a's arm keyed "_kiro.dev/session/update" (convert/kiro.rs:681) is dead, echoes fall to the existing kiro.dev/session/update arm (line 424) and are dropped. Repro: .k1b-steering/probe.rs + wire_shim.py + oracle-wire-capture.log.
acceptance_criteria: A live mid-turn steer against kiro 2.7.0+ produces SteeringQueued in cyril (no -32601); a captured _kiro.dev/session/update steering echo yields SteeringQueued/Consumed/Cleared. Regression fence: wire-method test asserting the bytes are _session/steer (single _) and a converter test keyed on the stripped method.
notes: Closed: Fixed by PR #20 (merged ad71d4d): outbound bridge.rs now sends unprefixed session/steer[/clear] (single _ on wire); inbound steering echoes folded into the kiro.dev/session/update arm; dead _kiro.dev arm deleted. Verified end-to-end vs kiro 2.8.0 (.k1b-steering/fix-verification.log). Follow-up commit e4ac8e6 added the steer_methods_are_unprefixed regression fence.

title: KAS-2b — Live context-usage breakdown bar (session_info_update:context_usage)
description:
On KAS (v3), the per-category context breakdown is PUSHED proactively on the wire — so cyril can render a live categorized context bar (Files / Tools / Prompts / Responses) in the toolbar WITHOUT the user running /context. On v1/v2 the breakdown only exists as the pulled /context command response; the streaming _kiro.dev/metadata notification there carries only the scalar contextUsagePercentage.

WIRE SHAPE (probe-verified, kiro-cli 2.8.0, 2026-06-19, live KAS turn):
- session/update -> session_info_update, _meta.kiro.kind == 'context_usage', payload {usagePercentage, breakdown?} (covenant ContextUsageBreakdown).
- breakdown = 5 buckets {contextFiles, tools, kiroResponses, yourPrompts, sessionFiles}, each {tokens, percent, items?}.
- ~6 frames pushed per turn.

CONSTRAINTS THAT SHAPE THE UI (all probe-verified):
1. tools bucket is AGGREGATE-ONLY — items is structurally absent (live: {tokens:33703, percent:3.4}). Only contextFiles/sessionFiles ever carry items[]. Do NOT build per-tool drill-down; the wire cannot support it on either engine.
2. breakdown is OPTIONAL PER FRAME — some context_usage frames carry usagePercentage only (breakdown absent). Retain last non-absent; treat absence as 'no update', not 'cleared' (same discipline as the effort field).
3. cyril's existing /context path (kiro.dev/commands/execute) is REJECTED by KAS (-32603 'no persistence classification'). Must read the streaming notification, not the command surface.

MAPPING: breakdown is richer than cyril's flat ContextUsage/TokenCounts — either map usagePercentage to ContextUsage + extend, or model the breakdown as a new type. The itemized buckets can reuse the existing render helper append_context_items (crates/cyril/src/app.rs:900), today wired only to the pulled /context path.

DEPENDS ON: KAS-2a (walking skeleton — turn renders+ends via session_info_update:turn_end; not yet filed). Part of the KAS-2 _kiro/* dialect track, anchored on KAS-0 (cyril-atjw).

REFS: probe experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-context-breakdown-2.8.0.py; ROADMAP KAS-2b (docs/ROADMAP.md:179).
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine, cyril renders a live categorized context bar fed by session_info_update:context_usage frames — no /context command issued.
* Per-frame 'breakdown absent' is handled: last breakdown retained, not cleared.
* tools bucket renders as an aggregate row (no per-tool UI); contextFiles/sessionFiles render itemized when items[] is present.
* v1/v2 engine behavior is unchanged (still uses the pulled /context command response).
* State + render tests cover (a) a breakdown-absent frame and (b) the aggregate-only tools bucket.
notes:
2026-06-21 — Re-confirmed the 5-bucket breakdown independently (experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-context-shows-steering.py): contextFiles itemized the file the agent read; tools aggregate-only (33703 tokens, no items); the v2 /context (kiro.dev/commands/execute) rejected. RELEVANT NEGATIVE for this bar: the breakdown has NO steering bucket and does not itemize steering docs. Steering is a SEPARATE channel (_kiro/steering/documents_changed catalog + session_info_update:steering_inclusion) — so do NOT try to source an 'active rules/steering' view from the context bar; it isn't there. See memory reference_kiro_kas_filematch_steering_source.

Closed: Implemented and merged via PR #33 (merge 9aca2fe); follow-up review fixes in 56f57ec. Deferred items tracked: cyril-1116 (per-file drill-in), cyril-mdbp (narrow-terminal overflow), cyril-cx27 (SessionController lifecycle divergence).

title: acp 0.10.2 hard-fails session/request_permission on unknown PermissionOptionKind — release-audit watch item
description: Discovered by the cyril-qo13 design falsifier (probe_qo13_unknown_option_kind_parse): acp::PermissionOptionKind has no #[serde(other)] catch-all, so a request_permission whose options[] carries a kind outside {allow_once, allow_always, reject_once, reject_always} fails deserialization inside the acp crate -- the agent gets a JSON-RPC error and cyril's overlay never opens. Same class as the SessionUpdate hard-fail documented in CLAUDE.md ACP notes. Consequence: if a future kiro/KAS release introduces a new option kind, cyril's permission handling breaks at parse, upstream of convert/. WATCH: each release wire-audit should grep new traces for unseen 'kind' values in permission options; also re-check on any agent-client-protocol crate upgrade whether the enum gained a catch-all (the sentinel test in crates/cyril-core/src/protocol/convert/probe_qo13.rs flips if parsing starts succeeding). The dead '_ =>' defensive arm in to_permission_options only becomes live if the crate adds variants.

title: Surface Kiro sticky-default side effect: /model and /effort rewrite the user's global defaults
description:
2.12.3 audit finding (docs/kiro-2.12.3-wire-audit.md): Kiro's ACP model/effort command handlers have ALWAYS (>=2.11.0, probably older) auto-persisted the selection to user-global settings — chat.defaultModel and chat.modelDefaults.<modelId>.output_config.effort. Every later session (cyril AND the Kiro TUI) inherits it. 2.12.3 adds opt-out keys chat.disableAutoDefaultModel / chat.disableAutoDefaultEffort, honored on the ACP path too; <=2.12.1 ignores them.

Runtime evidence: experiments/conductor-spike/probe-sticky-defaults-2.12.3.py + logs/sticky-probe-2.1{1.0,2.1,2.3}-20260715.log. Response message signals it: 'Model changed to X (saved as default)' vs no suffix when opted out.

What cyril should do (pick during triage): (a) document in README/help that /model + /effort are user-global sticky on Kiro; (b) optionally detect the '(saved as default)' suffix or settings state and hint the opt-out keys; (c) nothing in code — docs only. NOT a parser/wire gap; shapes unchanged.

title: Consolidate conversation-theme fixture test plumbing
description: Review finding #18: six test sites duplicate TSV parsing, byte encoding, scene construction, tool fixtures, and ANSI normalization. Consolidate mechanical plumbing without weakening per-widget shape fences.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shared parsing/encoding/fixture helpers have one implementation.
- [ ] Per-widget shape tests remain independent and readable.
- [ ] Projection tables have a single authoritative source.

title: Make the hooks panel responsive
description:
## What to build

Render hooks through a responsive table that keeps all hook meaning accessible on narrow terminals. Wide views retain Trigger, Command, and Matcher columns; narrow views stack matcher information beneath the command instead of dropping it.

## Blocked by

title: KAS terminal registry: kill/release during pending wait_for_exit are silent no-ops; release-then-wait resurrects the freed id
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review (perf/reliability lens) — untracked by any existing terminal issue (distinct from cyril-3lh8 orphan-on-cancel, cyril-ba5x Shutdown orphans, cyril-r3t6 pipe drain).

wait() takes the Child out of the registry (Entry::Running(None)) and awaits wait_with_output(). The acp rpc layer spawns every inbound request as its own concurrent spawn_local task (verified in agent-client-protocol-0.10.2 rpc.rs handle_incoming), so terminal/kill or terminal/release arriving WHILE wait is pending hits take_child -> Taken::InFlight, and both handlers use `if let Taken::Child(..)` — the InFlight case FALLS THROUGH and returns Ok without signaling the process:

- kill (terminal_io.rs:213): returns KillTerminalResponse having killed nothing. The module's own docs say KAS calls wait immediately after create (:16-17) and that kill-then-read-partial-output is KAS's command-timeout pattern (:205-208) — so EVERY kill arrives during a pending wait. Failure: KAS runs a hung command via create+wait; on its timeout it sends kill -> cyril replies Ok, process keeps running; the pending wait never resolves; the turn hangs (or the hung child leaks as a live orphan forever).
- release (terminal_io.rs:188): same fall-through (child not killed), then :200 unconditionally remove()s the id. When the still-pending wait() later completes, :151 insert()s an Exited entry BACK under the released id — violating the released-id -> -32602 contract and leaking the entry + captured output for the life of the bridge.

Root cause is one spot (the InFlight fall-through). A fix needs a kill signal that does not require owning the Child — e.g. retain the pid at create, or store a kill handle before wait takes the Child. The single-request-at-a-time tests never exercise the concurrent case.
acceptance_criteria: kill during a pending wait actually terminates the process and the pending wait resolves with the killed status; release during a pending wait kills the child and the id stays unknown (-32602) after the wait completes (no re-insert); concurrency tests added for kill-during-wait and release-during-wait on a current_thread runtime.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #42 (02718e0). Repro: kill_during_pending_wait_terminates_child + release_during_pending_wait_frees_id (current_thread concurrency fences).

title: KAS-4 — Model the 4 new initialize capability keys: executionTargets/sessionSources/sessionListScopes/sourceProviders (2.12.3)
description: KAS 0.17.2 (kiro-cli 2.12.3) adds four _meta.kiro capability keys on the initialize response: executionTargets (["local"] | +"cloud-sandbox"), sessionSources (["local"] | +"remote"), sessionListScopes (["workspace"] | +"user"), sourceProviders (bool; gates _kiro/sourceProviders/list + listResources). Standalone CLI reports the dormant local-only values. Per the model-full-wire-surface rule, cyril's KAS handshake types (KAS-1/KAS-2 lane) must model or explicit-ignore these. They are the advertisement surface for Kiro cloud/remote agents (remote session store, cloud-sandbox relay, GITHUB/GITLAB/MIDWAY source-provider catalog). Reference: docs/kiro-2.12.3-wire-audit.md; capture experiments/conductor-spike/logs/kas-host-init-2.12.3-20260715.json.

title: KAS context breakdown: per-file items[] drill-in (deferred from KAS-2b aggregate bar)
description: KAS context_usage frames carry items[] on the contextFiles + sessionFiles buckets (probe-verified 2.10.0, .cyril-5et2/context_usage_raw.json); tools/yourPrompts/kiroResponses are aggregate-only (no items). KAS-2b (cyril-5et2) renders only the aggregate category bar and deliberately does NOT model or render the per-file items[]. A follow-up can surface a per-file drill-in for contextFiles/sessionFiles under KAS, reusing the v2 /context append_context_items render helper. Out of scope for the KAS-2b bar; this is the tracker for the deferred drill-in.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — SoC CORRECTION, reword scope: do NOT reuse app.rs append_context_items (app.rs:1017-1052). It lives in the App binary and parses raw serde_json::Value from the v2 commands/execute response; reusing it for KAS either re-serializes typed data back to JSON or leaks raw KAS JSON past convert into App — both boundary regressions (CLAUDE.md: App must not parse JSON), and it couples a KAS feature to the v2 response-format dialect. Note ContextBucket deliberately omits an items field with a comment naming this issue — the no-drill-in invariant is type-enforced (types/session.rs:264-269).
Right shape: typed ContextItem (name/tokens/percent/flags) on ContextBucket in cyril-core, parsed in convert/kas.rs, rendered in cyril-ui. Reuse the presentation FORMAT, not the function. Bonus: creates the destination for eventually migrating the v2 /context formatting out of app.rs — shrinking a pre-existing App-layer violation instead of doubling down on it.

2026-07-02 — Live per-file data confirmed from the KAS trace (kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl, context_usage). contextFiles.items[] carried 47 entries, each {name, uri, tokens, matched, percent, progressivelyLoaded}. The typed ContextItem should include progressivelyLoaded (bool) — progressively-loaded skills show tokens:0 + progressivelyLoaded:true (ties to _kiro/progressive_context). So the fields to model: name, uri, tokens, percent, matched, progressivelyLoaded. Data is fully present on the wire; this is purely convert+render work. Breakdown buckets seen: contextFiles (has items[]), sessionFiles, kiroResponses, tools, yourPrompts (only contextFiles carries per-file items in this capture).

title: Absent contextUsagePercentage on kiro.dev/metadata stamps main context to 0.0 (sentinel fallback)
description: Discovered during cyril-fh06 (PR #54): real 2.4.1 captures contain v2 _kiro.dev/metadata frames with NO contextUsagePercentage (e.g. {"sessionId":…, "turnDurationMs":2281, "effort":"high"} in experiments/conductor-spike/trace-2.4.1-multi-subagent.jsonl). convert/kiro.rs warns and falls back to 0.0, so a main-session frame of this shape stamps the toolbar context-usage to 0.0% — a plausible-looking wrong value, violating the workspace 'errors are not default values' rule. Fix shape: make the notification field Option<f64> (absent = no-change, same retain-last discipline as effort) or skip the context update when the key is absent; keep metering/duration/effort applying. Left byte-identical in cyril-fh06 on purpose.
acceptance_criteria: A metadata frame without contextUsagePercentage leaves context usage unchanged while still applying its metering/duration/effort; a frame with the field still updates; regression test replays the captured no-context frame shape.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #56 (52fa590). Repro: convert::tests::metadata_without_context_retains_last_context (replays captured 2.4.1 duration/effort-only frame through to_ext_notification -> SessionController; failed red at Some(0.0) pre-fix). Companion fences: metadata_without_context_still_applies_metering_and_effort + cyril-ui toolbar retention test. context_usage is now Option<ContextUsage>; absent = retain-last, present-but-non-numeric warns with value.

title: Local gate never compiles kas-gated code — document/mandate --features kas in the standard verification loop
description: Surfaced by the 2026-07-04 bug sweep (cyril-lw67/cyril-0gke fixers): the CLAUDE.md-documented local gate (cargo test / cargo nextest run / cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings) never compiles feature-gated kas code (e.g. protocol/kas/terminal_io.rs), so kas modules are locally unlinted and untested unless the developer remembers --features kas. CI covers it via the dedicated KAS Feature job, but both sweep subagents had to independently discover and add 'cargo nextest run -p cyril-core --features kas' + matching clippy to their gates. Cheap fix: add the two --features kas commands to CLAUDE.md's Build & Test section (and any agent-facing gate checklists) so the local loop matches CI. Pre-existing; not introduced by the sweep PRs (#41/#42).

title: Add the remaining bundled theme palettes
description:
## What to build

Add the five approved palettes that complement Cyril Dark: Cyril Light, High Contrast Dark, High Contrast Light, Catppuccin Mocha, and Gruvbox Dark. Each palette must populate the established semantic roles and select a compatible syntax-theme component; selection and configuration remain out of scope.

## Blocked by

title: KAS host-io: translate WSL-internal paths (/home, /tmp) for a Windows host
description:
cyril's platform::path::wsl_to_win only translates /mnt/<drive> mount paths (/mnt/c/... -> C:\...). WSL-internal paths (/home/<user>, /tmp, /root) pass through unchanged. On a Windows host running `wsl kiro-cli acp`, a KAS workspace under the WSL-native filesystem makes fs/{read,write}_text_file callbacks carry /home/... paths.

After cyril-7bdu's to_native_checked fix these now fail HONESTLY as -32603 NotFound (has_root() accepts them, to_native leaves them unchanged, tokio::fs cannot open a WSL ext4 path from the Windows host) instead of a misleading 'must be absolute' -32602 -- but they still do not RESOLVE. Full Windows+KAS support for WSL-native workspaces needs \\wsl$\<distro>\... (or \\wsl.localhost\...) translation in platform::path.

Latent today: KAS is Linux-only in practice; /mnt/<drive> workspaces already work after the fix. Discovered during max-effort review of PR #34 (KAS-5a).
acceptance_criteria: wsl_to_win maps a WSL-internal path (e.g. /home/u/p/x.txt) to a \\wsl$\<distro>\... UNC path on Windows (no-op on Linux); round-trip + json-translation tests; distro-name resolution decided ($WSL_DISTRO_NAME / config); a KAS fs read+write against a WSL-native workspace succeeds on a Windows host.

title: Make crew status responsive
description:
## What to build

Present crew members and pending stages through a responsive display-only table when space permits, then collapse to a one-line status summary when height is constrained. The compact summary must preserve the distinction between a crew member and a pending stage.

## Blocked by

- cyril-dij8

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Standard layouts show deterministic crew rows with responsive name and status columns.
- [ ] Constrained layouts prioritize working, waiting, and completed counts over member names.
- [ ] Pending stages contribute to waiting work but are never labeled as crew members.
