title: Remove or honor ineffective UI configuration fields
description: Review finding #5: highlight_cache_size and stream_buffer_timeout_ms are serialized and documented but have no production consumers; defaults disagree with actual behavior. Choose removal/deprecation or wire them through the owning runtime components.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No documented UI config field is silently ignored.
- [ ] Compatibility behavior for existing config files is documented and tested.

title: Multi-client observer: count/echo steers cyril didn't originate (wire steering_queued)
description:
When cyril-7z7u makes the steer chip OPTIMISTIC (incremented at add_steer_echo / user-send) and stops incrementing on the wire SteeringQueued notification (to avoid double-counting cyril's own steers), a steer that ANOTHER client on the same session originates — surfaced only as a wire SteeringQueued with no local optimistic echo — will not be counted or echoed by cyril.

This is fine for cyril's single-client model TODAY. It becomes a gap if/when the multi-client observer direction lands (ROADMAP session-level workflows / _kiro/sessions/changed). At that point, reintroduce wire-SteeringQueued handling for steers WITHOUT a matching local echo (observer-originated), without double-counting cyril's own optimistic steers — likely needs a correlation key the wire doesn't currently provide (content is advisory).

REF: cyril-7z7u falsifiable-design (.cyril-7z7u/falsifiable-design.md), negative space.

title: AgentProcess stderr piped but never drained — >64KB cumulative stderr wedges the agent; crash diagnostics discarded
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review. AgentProcess::spawn sets .stderr(Stdio::piped()) but child.stderr is never taken or read anywhere (transport.rs:25; verified by grep — _child just holds it). Once the child writes ~64KB (Linux pipe buffer) cumulatively to stderr, its next stderr write BLOCKS the whole process -> the engine wedges mid-turn with no error anywhere (and per cyril-l7tw, the wedge is silent).

Predates KAS but KAS makes it material: the engine is `node --experimental-wasm-modules` (Node emits runtime warnings to stderr by default) running an 801MB TypeScript agent whose logging/tracebacks go to stderr, over sessions that can run for hours. Even below the wedge threshold, everything KAS prints to stderr — including the reason for a spawn-then-immediate-crash — is discarded, so 'ACP initialization failed' is all the user ever gets.

Fix shape: take() stderr and spawn a drain task on the LocalSet (debug!-log lines; ring-buffer the tail so BridgeDisconnected reasons can include it — pairs with cyril-l7tw's visibility fix).
acceptance_criteria: An agent process writing >64KB to stderr does not stall (test with a chatty stub); drained stderr appears at debug level in cyril.log; the last N stderr lines are available to the disconnect path for actionable BridgeDisconnected reasons.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #41 (5185b2f + bce5472 bounded-read review fix). Repro: chatty_stderr_does_not_wedge_agent_process + newline_free_stderr_flood_drains_in_bounded_chunks; tail fences stderr_tail_keeps_only_last_n_lines / stderr_tail_snapshot_holds_final_lines_after_exit.

title: Model picker: surface per-model capability (hasEffort + credit rate) from KAS model options
description: KAS model options (session/new configOptions[model].options[]._meta.kiro) carry structured per-model metadata cyril currently DROPS: hasEffort (bool — does the model support thinking-effort levels), rateMultiplier (numeric credit rate), rateUnit, effortSchemaPath. cyril's convert layer discards _meta and CommandOption only has {label,value,description,group,is_current}; hasEffort is consumed nowhere. Goal: parse hasEffort + rateMultiplier from model options and surface in the model picker — a 'supports effort' badge and a credit-cost hint — so the user sees which models are thinking-capable before selecting, and optionally gate the /effort control on the selected model's hasEffort. ENGINE ASYMMETRY (diffed 2026-07-02): hasEffort is KAS-ONLY on the wire. v2 session/new models.availableModels are bare {modelId,name,description}; v2 encodes the rate as a display string in the picker group ('2.20x credits', == KAS rateMultiplier 2.2) and has NO hasEffort — so on v2, effort-capability must keep being inferred from whether /effort is registered in commands/available (2.4.1). So: KAS-first; v2 gets the credit hint via group only. NOTE: showing the CURRENT effort level is ALREADY implemented (toolbar ◇ {effort} from MetadataUpdated.effort) — this issue is about the model-picker capability display, not the current-level indicator.
acceptance_criteria: Model picker shows an effort-capable badge (from KAS hasEffort) + credit-rate hint; /effort optionally gated on selected-model hasEffort (KAS) / commands/available presence (v2); v2 degrades gracefully (no hasEffort on wire).

title: Preserve speaker identity in ANSI-16 projection
description: Review finding #6: nearest-RGB ANSI-16 projection maps user and system to identical Gray and agent to the same DarkGray used by muted roles. Introduce semantic projection constraints before ANSI-16 mode is activated.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] User, agent, and system remain distinguishable in ANSI-16 mode.
- [x] Projection tests and independent oracle encode the semantic constraint.
notes: Closed: Implemented by 15b4ffb, 81a0a6d, and 9fa4251. ANSI-16 speaker slots are LightBlue/LightGreen/LightMagenta; muted-role collisions are rejected; resolver and five runtime consumer paths are independently fenced. Full workspace fmt, strict Clippy, tests, C0-C7 oracles, and resolution budget pass.

title: Theme-source scanner breaks on CRLF checkouts — main's Windows CI red since 2026-07-12
description:
conversation_theme_sources.rs audit_source computes token offsets with 'source_offset += line.len() + 1', which under-counts CRLF files by one byte per line (lines() strips 
). signed_ranges come from source.find() (true byte offsets), so on Windows runners (no .gitattributes, Git-for-Windows autocrlf) every token deep in a file drifts below its signed exemption range: production_highlight_is_clean false-flags the four Color::* arms INSIDE color_to_rgb (highlight.rs:198-201). Reproduced deterministically by CRLF-converting the source and re-running the algorithm (LF: clean; CRLF: exactly CI's four findings). Main red since the theme-work push 2026-07-12 (run 29182785122); first surfaced on PR #51 which inherited it via merge. Fix: normalize 
 -> 
 in production_source before auditing + a CRLF regression test on audit_source.

title: KAS-8 — Render _kiro/system/notify {level, message} instead of dropping it
description:
KAS 0.17.2 (kiro-cli 2.12.3) adds connection-scoped notification _kiro/system/notify {level, message}, wired to the agent's onDelayMessage hook (system/delay messages, e.g. throttling or long-wait notices). Same family as cyril-3zy4 (_kiro/error/rate_limit) and cyril-3ald (_kiro/safety/*): a KAS-aware cyril should render these as system chat lines with level-appropriate styling, not silently drop. Note _kiro/system/ is a PREFIX namespace (CONNECTION_SCOPED_NOTIFICATION_PREFIXES = ['_kiro/sessions/', '_kiro/system/']) — handle unknown _kiro/system/* kinds gracefully. Reference: docs/kiro-2.12.3-wire-audit.md.

TRIGGER CONFIRMED (bundle read 2026-07-15): the onDelayMessage hook is fired by QClientRetryStrategy during model-request backoff — NOT remote/cloud-only. Two canned cases: delayMs>WARN threshold -> (DELAY_WARN_MESSAGE, level='warning'); delayMs>INFO threshold -> (DELAY_INFO_MESSAGE, level='info'). So level values seen = info|warning, messages are canned 'model is taking longer than usual'-type strings. Fires on ordinary slow/throttled LOCAL KAS turns, so any KAS-aware cyril will receive it in normal use — not an exotic remote path. Emit = connection.extNotification('_kiro/system/notify', {level, message}). New in KAS 0.17.2 (0 in 2.12.1).
notes: Closed: Shipped: PR #60 merged to main. KAS _kiro/system/notify {level, message} now renders as level-prefixed system message. 8 AC fences pass: converter (7), UI (2 includes busy-preservation). Fixed in-branch: simplified Unknown-level arm per bot review. Filed: none.

title: KAS auth: --auth=machine / KIRO_API_KEY bypass _kiro/auth/getAccessToken — simpler fallback path
description: Static analysis of @kiro/agent (present in 0.3.299 AND 0.8.0, i.e. not new but previously undocumented for us): acp-server.js selectAuthProvider priority = (1) KIRO_API_KEY env -> EnvAuthProvider, (2) --auth=acp-callback -> AcpCallbackAuthProvider (current cyril path, host-mediated refresh, profileArn gotcha), (3) --auth=machine -> FileAuthProvider plain-text token at tokenPath or /tmp/access_token, (4) explicit tokenPath JSON, (5) default ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json. Modes 1/3/4/5 never call _kiro/auth/getAccessToken. Option value: degraded-mode fallback when the callback path breaks (e.g. profileArn rotation bugs), and a simpler harness for probes. Note kiro-cli's own spawn of KAS may pin --auth=acp-callback; direct-spawn is where this pays off.
acceptance_criteria: Documented in KAS integration notes; optionally a bridge config knob to direct-spawn KAS with --auth=machine for diagnostics
notes: Closed: Already covered: launch-contract memory (2026-06-21 server-side ground truth) documents selectAuthProvider priority incl. --auth=machine/KIRO_API_KEY, and the no-callback free path was empirically confirmed on 2.8.1; cyril-taba tracks the practical token-staleness gap. 2.11.0 audit confirmed the auth layer is byte-identical in 0.8.0 — no new work item.

title: KAS: consume turn_completion summary + focus_update + _kiro/governance/state (currently all dropped)
description:
Systematic pass over the live KAS trace (experiments/conductor-spike/kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl) found 3 agent->client message kinds cyril drops that carry genuinely useful UI signal:
1. session_info_update::turn_completion — {elapsedTime(ms), status(success|aborted), promptTurnSummaries:[{unit:'credit', unitPlural, usage(float), usedTools:[...]}]}. RICHER than cyril's flat MetadataUpdated metering (single credit number): per-turn cost + duration + the full list of tools the turn used (e.g. turn 2 = 22.17 credits, 769686ms, [user_input, invoke_sub_agent, grep_search, read_file, report_progress, subagent_response, ...]). Could power a per-turn cost/activity summary line. cyril's session_info_to_notification (kas.rs) handles only turn_end + context_usage; turn_completion falls to _ => None.
2. session_info_update::focus_update — {focus:{title}, title} = the agent's current focus ('Start a new spec called multiple-notes...'). A 'what it's working on' status-line indicator. Dropped.
3. _kiro/governance/state — {isEnterprise, features:{mcpEnabled, webToolsEnabled, usageAnalytics, contentCollection, promptLogging, codeReferenceTracker, autonomousAgents}}. Org/enterprise feature gating: which capabilities the org allows. Advisory (backend enforces) but a real UI-gating input (hide web-tools/MCP affordances when disabled, show content-collection indicator). Dropped.
All three drop safely today (no crash) — this is opt-in display work when the KAS converter (KAS-2/KAS-4) is fleshed out. STRUCTURAL NOTE: KAS session_info_update kinds double-encode (typed sub-object + flattened fields); read the flat _meta.kiro.X consistently. Full inventory in the trace companion doc.
acceptance_criteria: KAS converter maps turn_completion -> a per-turn summary notification (cost + usedTools + elapsed); focus_update -> a status indicator; _kiro/governance/state -> feature flags the UI can gate on. Each degrades gracefully (v2 has no equivalent). Reads the flat _meta.kiro.* encoding.

title: KAS terminals orphaned on Shutdown/bridge death — no kill_on_drop, no Shutdown-arm sweep
description: Found by the 2026-07-01 issue-set reliability review (grounded in code, not speculative): BridgeCommand::Shutdown aborts only prompt_task (bridge.rs:1363-1365); TerminalRegistry children are spawned WITHOUT kill_on_drop (terminal_io.rs:85-104), and when run_loop breaks, dropping the LocalSet kills nothing — live terminal children survive cyril exiting entirely. cyril-3lh8 covers only the session/cancel + turn-end reaping paths; this is the bridge-LIFETIME path (Shutdown, bridge thread death, app exit mid-command). Cheapest fix: .kill_on_drop(true) on the Command at create (also backstops the cyril-3lh8/cyril-r3t6 cancel-wedge interaction) + an explicit registry sweep in the Shutdown arm. Coordinate with cyril-3lh8's session-keyed reaping so the sweep reuses the same registry mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: No KAS-spawned terminal child outlives the cyril process: exiting cyril (or bridge Shutdown) while a long-running terminal command is in flight kills the child. Verified with a sleep-60 terminal command + a process-tree check after exit.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #43 (a875cba + 8cc9ae7 portable fence probe). Repro: dropped_registry_kills_running_terminal + dropped_inflight_wait_kills_terminal. kill_on_drop alone met criteria — no Shutdown sweep, no g9vt seam wiring needed.

title: Remove/align unread SessionController.steering_depth (diverges from optimistic UiState chip)
description:
SessionController.steering_depth (cyril-core/session.rs) is a wire-driven steer counter (SteeringQueued +1, SteeringConsumed -1, Cleared/SessionCreated =0; NO turn-end reset) with ZERO non-test readers (the chip reads UiState.steering_queued via toolbar.rs:80).

cyril-7z7u makes UiState.steering_queued OPTIMISTIC (counted at add_steer_echo / user-send), so it no longer mirrors the wire-driven steering_depth — the "in lockstep with SessionController.steering_depth" comments (state.rs ~538/1909) are now stale. Harmless today (steering_depth is unread), but it's dead/divergent code.

Action: either (a) delete steering_depth + its tests (it's unused), or (b) make it optimistic to match if a future consumer needs a core-side steer count. Decide when touched. Found during cyril-7z7u checkpointed-build.
notes: DONE in feat/cyril-vgcm-steer-clear slice 1 (2026-07-14): SessionController.steering_depth deleted (write-only, zero readers — grep-verified; stale state.rs comment rewritten). steering_unsupported kept, now steer-only (clear--32601 must not set it, vgcm C12). Close when the PR merges.

title: KAS host callbacks gated by cargo feature, not bound engine — implement ADR-0001 capability accessors or amend the ADR
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review (SoC lens). In a --features kas build running the V2 engine, KiroClient still answers kiro/auth/getAccessToken with the real token and answers fs/* and terminal/* callbacks if the subprocess sends them (client.rs:192-281) — despite V2Engine advertising no such capabilities, and despite ADR-0001's design intent that these are surfaces 'KAS implements and v2 does not.' The cargo feature quietly replaced the bound engine as the availability gate for host callbacks.

ADR-0001's consequences also promise the capability sub-trait mechanism ('the accessor pattern is introduced in KAS-1, where AuthResponder gets a real implementation and consumer') — no AuthResponder trait exists anywhere; auth landed as a static feature-gated handler, fs/terminal as feature-gated KiroClient overrides. The ADR text is stale.

Practical risk is LOW today (the subprocess is kiro-cli; v2 never sends these callbacks — the code says so at client.rs:23-24,180). But the next capability (KAS-7 hooks, _kiro/safety/*, cyril-3ald) will copy whatever precedent exists, so resolve before then. DISTINCT from cyril-g9vt: that issue is about the ADR-0004 stage-interception seam; this one is engine-vs-feature gating of responder availability.

Cheapest fix short of sub-traits: have the responders consult the bound engine (KiroClient already holds Rc<dyn Engine>) before answering — refuse with method-not-found when the engine does not advertise the capability. Either way, amend ADR-0001's consequences to match what shipped.
acceptance_criteria: A kas-feature build running the v2 engine refuses getAccessToken / fs/* / terminal/* (method-not-found or equivalent), test-fenced; OR ADR-0001 is amended to document feature-level gating as the accepted design with the trade-off stated. Stale AuthResponder consequence text corrected in either outcome.

title: Steering echoes silently dead: backend rollout (Jun 17 - Jul 9 2026) renamed v2 echoes to AgentExecution*; KAS kinds unmapped
description: Live-probed 2026-07-09 on 2.12.0 (.cyril-vgcm/findings.md F2/F3/F8). A BACKEND rollout between 2026-06-17 (K1b capture, binary 2.8.0: steering_queued{message} live) and 2026-07-09 (archived 2.7.0 binary now emits the new family) renamed the v2 steering echoes for ALL binary versions: steering_queued{message} -> AgentExecutionUserMessageQueued{messageId,content}; steering_consumed -> AgentExecutionSteeringInjected{messageId,content}; steering_cleared -> AgentExecutionUserMessageCleared{messageIds}. NOT a binary change (strings rename-table dating was a red herring; wire = binary x backend). convert/kiro.rs matches only the old literals -> unknown-variant Err -> every echo dropped; the optimistic steer chip increments at send and never reconciles (phantom chip on EVERY steer today; overlaps cyril-nvmh paths b/c). KAS echoes ride session_info_update _meta.kiro.kind steering_queued/steering_injected/steering_cleared, which convert/kas.rs also drops (matches only turn_end/context_usage). Fix: converter re-base handling BOTH v2 literal families (rollouts can stage/revert) + KAS kinds. Being fixed on the cyril-vgcm branch.
notes: FIXED by feat/cyril-vgcm-steer-clear (2026-07-14): converters re-based on the live wire — kiro.rs handles BOTH v2 echo families (old family kept as rollback insurance), kas.rs maps the three steering kinds. Echo pipeline live again on both engines; close when the PR merges.

title: KAS host-io loop-mediation seam (ADR-0004 gate/transform point for fs+terminal)
description:
Deferred from KAS-5a (cyril-7bdu) during checkpointed-build. ADR-0004 decided that server->client host-io requests (fs, terminal) route THROUGH the bridge run_loop's host-io arm so the engine's capability sub-traits / a cyril-stages gate can observe/transform them. KAS-5a instead resolves fs DIRECTLY in #[cfg(feature=kas)] KiroClient overrides (kas::host_io::{read,write}_text_file) because: (1) the loop seam has no consumer yet — no stages gate exists (the codebase's engine.rs:12 rule says capability sub-traits land WITH their first consumer, not as consumer-less stubs); (2) routing through run_loop forces a #[cfg]'d run_loop parameter (can't cleanly cfg a single fn param) — either ship HostIoRequest+channel un-gated in every v2 build (violates ADR-0002 'default build links no KAS code') or maintain two cfg'd run_loop signatures; (3) the non-blocking invariant (KAS-5a claim C4) is already satisfied WITHOUT the loop arm — the acp ClientSideConnection spawns each inbound request as its own spawn_local task (rpc.rs:272, wired bridge.rs:390), so a direct async tokio::fs resolve is off-loop and never starves the loop.

WHEN TO BUILD THIS: when the first real consumer of the seam lands — a cyril-stages host-io gate (audit/policy/transform of file reads+writes+shell execs, ADR-0003/Phase 2) or KAS-7 hooks. At that point, move fs+terminal resolution behind a HostIoRequest channel + run_loop arm so the consumer can intercept, and resolve ADR-0002's cfg-threading (likely: gate the whole host-io module + a kas-only run_loop variant, or accept un-gated host-io once it's no longer kas-only). Until then, direct-resolve in the cfg'd overrides is the right altitude.

REFS: ADR-0004 (docs/adr/0004); .cyril-7bdu/falsifiable-design.md claim C3 + Negative-space #3; KAS-5a cyril-7bdu.

title: KAS-nhzw follow-up: overlay workspace kiro-cli settings onto global for _meta.kiro.settings

Scope: confirm the path kiro-cli --workspace writes, read it if present, deep-merge over global before marshaling, add a claim+falsifier (workspace key overrides global). Discovered-from cyril-nhzw.

title: KAS-1 follow-up: auto-refresh kiro token file on stale before getAccessToken reply (wrapper mode)
description: KAS-1 Part B (cyril-evwh) ships the wrapper auth responder reading ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json -> {accessToken,expiresAt,profileArn}. In WRAPPER mode KAS injects --auth=acp-callback and stops self-refreshing the file, so a STALE token (expiresAt <= now+180s) would be a known-bad reply. KAS-1 returns an actionable Err on stale (user re-runs kiro-cli login). THIS = automate it: on stale, trigger kiro-cli's own refresh (PROBE candidates: kiro-cli whoami/profile/user; OR the file-auth refresh path KAS itself uses) so the reply stays valid >now+3min WITHOUT reimplementing OIDC. Falsifier C9 (.cyril-evwh/design.md): force/await stale file, run candidate refresh, assert expiresAt advances >now+3min. DEFERRED from KAS-1: token was fresh during the build (staleness needs ~1h). is_stale() unit logic already lands in kas::auth; only the refresh trigger + live verification deferred here.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — four robustness requirements the implementation must state explicitly (the shell-out is inherently fragile: it relies on an undocumented side effect kiro could remove any release; probe-gating via falsifier C9 is the right mitigation and stays):
(1) tokio::process only, never std::process — the responder runs on the single-threaded bridge (auth.rs:52-54 non-blocking rationale applies doubly to spawning a process).
(2) A timeout — kiro-cli can hang on network.
(3) A single-flight guard so concurrent getAccessToken requests don't spawn N refresh processes.
(4) NEVER trust the exit code — re-read and re-validate via the existing read_token_file + is_stale after the refresh attempt; treat the shell-out purely as a best-effort trigger.
The safe fallback (actionable -32000 Err on stale, auth.rs:159-164) stays load-bearing: refresh failure degrades to today's behavior, never a known-bad reply.

REFRAMED BY cyril-dcc6 DESIGN (2026-07-04): once dcc6 lands, the getAccessToken responder reads the CLI sqlite store (auth_kv token + state profileArn), not ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json — so this issue's 'auto-refresh the token FILE' premise is obsolete. What remains in scope here: the STALE-SQLITE-TOKEN policy. The responder keeps its is_stale() 3-min-buffer gate and errors actionably ('run kiro-cli login'); cyril cannot auto-refresh without consuming the CLI's single-use refresh token (see reference_kiro_kas_launch_contract — refresh-less relay is the safe contract). Likely resolution: early stale detection + a re-login affordance in the UI, not refresh.

PARTIALLY ABSORBED BY PR-39 REVIEW FIXES (2026-07-04): the 'early stale detection' half is now implemented — the free-path spawn gate (store_unservable_reason, auth.rs) applies the same is_stale check as the responder, so a known-expired login fails the spawn actionably instead of dying on the first callback (review finding F3; decision log .cyril-dcc6/review-decisions.md). REMAINING SCOPE: the mid-session case — a UI re-login affordance when a callback returns the -32000 stale error (pairs with cyril-l7tw's App-notification work).

description:

title: K1b cross-turn steer echo/chip turn-end semantics unverified (needs backend probe)
description: Code-review (cyril-bm1j, xhigh) found a latent ambiguity in K1b's turn-end handling. TurnCompleted resets the chip (steering_queued=0, claim C9) but leaves any still-Queued SteerEcho in scrollback (negative-space #6). If the backend PERSISTS an end-of-turn-un-consumed steer and drains it on the NEXT turn (the idle-steer capture .k1b-steering/idle-steer-wire-capture.log shows steers survive the idle->turn boundary, suggesting it does), then: (a) the chip UNDER-counts a still-pending steer after turn-end, and (b) the next turn's SteeringConsumed FIFO-flips the stale prior-turn Queued echo. If instead the backend DROPS un-consumed steers at turn-end, the current reset is right but the echo should be finalized too. Both directions bake in an unverified assumption, so the fix was deferred. ACTION: probe whether a steer queued at end-of-turn (no further tool boundary) drains on the next turn; then either (1) stop resetting the chip at turn-end and let it drain naturally, or (2) finalize leftover Queued echoes at turn-end to match the reset. The cross-SESSION variant was already fixed (session_created_finalizes_prior_session_steer_echoes); this is the same mechanism across a TURN boundary within one session.
design:
Falsifiable design: .cyril-7z7u/falsifiable-design.md. Scope = OPTIMISTIC CHIP. Cheapest falsifier passed (claim 2 echo-flip preserved, consumed_flips_oldest_queued_echo green).

FIX (crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs only): make steering_queued optimistic, in lockstep with the echo, so invariant steering_queued == #SteerEcho{Queued}.
- add_steer_echo: +1 (NEW)
- SteeringConsumed: -1 + flip oldest Queued->Applied (kept)
- SteeringQueued (wire): no count change (was +1; REMOVED — avoid double-count)
- TurnCompleted: no reset (was =0; REMOVED — late steer's count/echo must survive)
- SteeringUnsupported: =0 (NEW — clear the optimistic count)
- SteeringCleared / SessionCreated: =0 (kept)

SUBTRACTIVE: removes "count==0 after TurnCompleted" (still-holds claim 3: drains via consumed) + "every increment has a wire match" (still-holds claim 4: wire queued must not double-count). echo logic (flip_queued_steer_echoes) UNCHANGED.

REWRITES 2 existing tests that lock the old model: turn_completed_resets_steering_queued (claim 3), steering_queue_mirror (claims 4/6/7). 9 claims total, each a state test fence.

Negative space: doesn't count observer-originated steers (cyril-8lfs); doesn't finalize echo at turn-end (rejects issue option b — probe shows late steer still pending).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (worktree fix/cyril-7z7u-cross-turn-steer-semantics). All gilfoyle gates green.
- crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs: steering_queued optimistic (add_steer_echo +1; SteeringConsumed -1; Cleared/Unsupported/SessionCreated =0; wire SteeringQueued no-op; TurnCompleted reset removed). Invariant: steering_queued == #SteerEcho{Queued}.
- 9 fences green incl. RED->GREEN rewrites of turn_completed_resets_steering_queued, steering_queue_mirror, steering_queued_exposed_via_tuistate_trait (locked the old model). Full workspace green, clippy -D clean, fmt clean.
- Discovered: SessionController.steering_depth = unread parallel wire counter, now divergent -> cyril-85py. Multi-observer steers -> cyril-8lfs.
Leave open pending review/merge.

title: KAS-5b — terminal host-callback responders (terminal/* + shell_type)
description:
KAS-5b: the terminal half of host I/O, split from cyril-7bdu (KAS-5a = fs read/write). Implement the typed acp::Client terminal methods cyril must answer when it advertises the terminal capability: create_terminal {sessionId, command, args, cwd}, terminal_output {sessionId, terminalId}, wait_for_terminal_exit {sessionId, terminalId}, release_terminal, kill_terminal (+ _kiro/terminal/shell_type {sessionId}). Wire shapes captured @ 2.10.0 in .cyril-7bdu/host_callbacks_2.10.0.json (bare ACP methods, every call carries sessionId, cwd absolute).

REUSES the KAS-5a seam (.cyril-7bdu/falsifiable-design.md): cap advertisement via Engine::client_capabilities() (add terminal:true), the ADR-0004 loop host-io mediation arm, the non-blocking-resolver invariant, and platform::path translation. Terminal STRESSES the non-blocking invariant hardest: a shell command can run up to 60s, so create_terminal must return a terminalId immediately and wait_for_terminal_exit must await via tokio::process (NOT a thread-pinning std::process wait) or it starves the single-threaded bridge runtime (rpc.rs spawns each request, but the runtime is current_thread+LocalSet). terminalIds must be unique across concurrent terminals; output/wait/release/kill on an unknown id must error, not panic. create with a nonexistent command must error, not panic the bridge.

DEPENDS ON KAS-5a (cyril-7bdu) for the seam. Run its own falsifiable-design pass before build.
notes: Closed: Merged in PR #36 (feat/kas-5-host-io -> main, c89a1f0). fs (5a) + terminal (5b) host-callback responders shipped; review fixes (stdin=null, logged reaps, wedge-on-error, cross-platform test gating) in a128633. CI green on Linux/macOS/Windows. Follow-ups remain as separate issues (3lh8, 0v42, 8tq6, g9vt, r3t6, 1rpv, ihj1).

title: Enter-while-busy silently drops the user's message (regression from cyril-84ca off-loop guard)
description:
Regression introduced by cyril-84ca (PR #22): pressing Enter (non-slash text) while session.status()==Busy silently drops the user's message.

ROOT CAUSE: app.rs submit_input() (crates/cyril/src/app.rs:640) has NO busy guard. On Enter it take_input()s (clears the box), add_user_message()s (commits the text to the visible chat), set_status(Busy)+set_activity(Sending), then sends BridgeCommand::SendPrompt. After cyril-84ca the bridge's one-turn guard rejects a second SendPrompt mid-turn with BridgeError{operation:'prompt', message:'a turn is already in progress'} (bridge.rs SendPrompt guard). So the message is committed to chat and the input cleared, but it is NEVER sent to the agent — only an error system message appears.

BEHAVIOR CHANGE: pre-cyril-84ca the blocked command loop QUEUED the second SendPrompt and ran it as a sequential second turn after the first completed (message eventually sent). Post-cyril-84ca it is rejected (message lost). Live on main in the window between cyril-84ca merging and cyril-bm1j (K1b) landing — cyril-84ca blocks cyril-bm1j, so this PR merges first.

SECOND AFFECTED PATH: dispatch_code_command's Prompt branch (app.rs:1043) programmatically emits SendPrompt with no busy check; a /code-triggered prompt arriving mid-turn is rejected the same way, and its already-committed user text is never sent.

UI INCOHERENCE: UiState's BridgeError handler (crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs:638) only add_system_message()s — it does NOT clear activity. So set_activity(Sending) from the rejected submit regresses the spinner from Streaming/ToolRunning back to 'Sending', the user sees 'prompt failed: a turn is already in progress', and then turn 1 keeps streaming — a sequence that did not occur pre-cyril-84ca.

PROPER FIX: this IS 'the latent second-prompt-mid-turn bug' that cyril-bm1j (K1b) is designed to fix by routing Enter-while-busy to SteerSession when status==Busy and input is non-slash. If the pre-K1b window matters, a minimal interim guard in submit_input (don't commit/clear + show a 'turn in progress' hint, or drop the second send before mutating UI) closes it without the full K1b UX.

Found by /code-review of PR #22.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting non-slash text while session.status()==Busy must not silently drop the message: either route it to SteerSession (K1b) or, at minimum, leave the input uncleared/uncommitted and surface a clear 'turn in progress' affordance, with no orphaned activity state (no spinner regression to Sending, no BridgeError-only dead end). The /code Prompt path (app.rs:1043) must likewise not lose its injected prompt mid-turn. Regression test: with a Busy session, submit Enter and assert the message is not lost and activity/status stay consistent.

title: Steering: KAS 0.8.0 adds _session/steer/clear (KAS-only; v2 lacks it) — adopt for queue-clear UX
description: kiro-cli 2.11.0 ships @kiro/agent 0.8.0 whose bundle adds _session/steer/clear alongside _session/steer (agent-side static diff vs 0.3.299; zero removals). The v2 Rust binary does NOT contain the full literal in 2.10.0 or 2.11.0 (only an unchanged pooled '_session/steer/c…' fragment), so treat clear as KAS-only until live-probed. Relevant to K1 steering: gives cyril a way to clear a queued steer instead of only adding. Runtime behavior unprobed — schema-accepted != functional; probe on KAS before building UX (send steer, then steer/clear, verify queue drains and no turn consumes the cleared steer).
acceptance_criteria: Live probe result recorded (KAS accepts/rejects + v2 -32601 confirmed); if functional, /steer clear or Esc-to-unqueue wired behind engine gate
notes:
LIVE-PROBED 2026-07-02 — CONFIRMED FUNCTIONAL on KAS 0.8.0, genuinely absent on 0.3.299 (control: PersistenceClassification error). Contract: request {sessionId} -> {cleared:true, messageIds:[...]}; broadcasts session_info_update kind=steering_cleared {messageIds}; empty queue=no-op []; missing/unknown sessionId -> -32603 w/details. Does NOT abort the active turn (only drops queued steers; distinct from cancel); bumps steering epoch (drops concurrent/in-flight _session/steer append) + persists reload-safe boundary marker. Live lifecycle: steer {sessionId, message} -> {queued:true, messageId:'steer-<uuid>'} + steering_queued; clear returned that exact id + steering_cleared. steer param is 'message' (string) NOT 'content[]'. End-to-end model-omits-instruction NOT verified (turn 403'd on stale SSO token; credential synth declined as out-of-scope) — epoch/buffer/notify lifecycle conclusive. v2 gate: v2 binary lacks the literal; send optimistically, treat -32601/PersistenceClassification as engine-unsupported. IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-14 on feat/cyril-vgcm-steer-clear — all 9 plan slices landed with per-slice gates (.cyril-vgcm/plan.md). C3-C13 fenced: kiro.rs converts BOTH v2 echo families (new AgentExecution* family live-captured; old kept per D1); kas.rs maps steering_queued/injected/cleared off verbatim 2.12.1 captures (steering_inclusion fenced as NOT a queue echo); UiState id-aware reconciliation (bind/consume/clear, 7z7u counter==chips oracle per step); /steer clear exact-match parse (D2); dispatch_clear_steer via steer_gate, zero optimistic mutation (D4); bridge clear--32601 -> SteeringClearUnsupported, never marks steering_unsupported (F5 fix, divergence pair fenced). steering_depth deleted (D5 -> closes cyril-85py). V2 LIVE SMOKE RE-RUN 2026-07-14 (post SSO re-login, kiro-cli 2.12.1) — the blocked final-gate leg is CLOSED: steer {queued:true} -> clear {cleared:true}, cleared KILO marker suppressed all 3 turns; echoes verbatim AgentExecutionUserMessageQueued{messageId,content} + AgentExecutionUserMessageCleared{messageIds:[exact id]} (+ SteeringInjected on the no-clear control) — F2/F7/F8 shapes reproduced, fences confirmed against the live backend. Control marker text did not land this run (model non-compliance, injected frame carried it verbatim — wire contract unaffected). See findings.md post-build section. title: KAS permission requests carry a stub toolCall (no rawInput) — approval preview must join the tracked tool_call by toolCallId
description:
From the 2026-07-02 KAS modes probe (docs/kiro-kas-modes-2.10.0.md, wire delta #3, dumps in experiments/conductor-spike/kas-modes-dumps/): on KAS 0.3.299 a session/request_permission's toolCall is a STUB — {status, title, toolCallId} only, no rawInput, no kind, no locations. If it reads the request payload, any content/diff preview is empty under KAS — wire the join by toolCallId (the tool_call notification always precedes the permission request in the captures). Not the same issue as cyril-qo13 (response-side option fidelity); this is request-side preview rendering.

title: KAS host-io: bounded read for fs/read_text_file (don't load whole file when line/limit set)
description:
kas::host_io::read_text_file uses tokio::fs::read_to_string, loading the ENTIRE file into memory before slice_lines selects [line, line+limit). For a large file with a small limit (KAS's chunked-read pattern), the whole file is read and allocated regardless of how few lines were requested -- a memory spike / OOM risk proportional to file size, not request size.

Consider tokio::io::BufReader + AsyncBufReadExt: skip(start), take(limit), stop early; keep the read-whole path for the None/None case. Must preserve slice_lines' byte-exact semantics (split_inclusive keeps trailing newline; CRLF intact) and the non-UTF-8 -> Err behavior. Per-callback, not a hot loop -- low priority. Discovered during review of PR #34 (KAS-5a).
acceptance_criteria: A read with line/limit on a large file allocates ~O(requested window), not O(file size); slice byte-exactness (trailing newline, CRLF) and non-UTF-8 -> Err preserved; whole-file (None/None) behavior unchanged; existing host_io read tests still pass.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) Do NOT use the lines() adapter — it strips the \n AND a trailing \r, destroying the documented byte-exact/CRLF semantics. Correct idiom: manual read_line (or read_until(b'\n')) loop — delimiter included, so \r\n survives; clear a reused buffer for the `start` skipped lines, accumulate `limit` lines, break on Ok(0). O(longest line + window) memory; no meaningfully simpler idiom exists.
(2) One acceptance criterion is unachievable by construction: 'non-UTF-8 -> Err' today validates the WHOLE file (read_to_string); a bounded read cannot validate bytes it never reads. Relax to Err-for-invalid-UTF-8-within-the-read-region and document the divergence (reading to EOF just to validate defeats the issue's purpose).
(3) line-beyond-EOF still yields "" under a streaming loop — matches current behavior, no special case needed.

title: KAS-2a — Walking skeleton: a plain KAS turn renders and ends
description:
The milestone that earns a demo: select KAS, authenticate, prompt, watch text + tool calls stream, turn completes, input is live again. The MINIMUM for a plain turn to render and end cleanly. Other _kiro/* surfaces (governance, mcp/status, powers, progressive_context) and the other session_info_update kinds all fire on a plain turn too, but cyril's existing unknown-variant debug! arm drops them gracefully — deferred to 2b-2d without hanging anything.

SCOPE (ROADMAP KAS-2a, docs/ROADMAP.md:165):
- Converter arm (engine-keyed, in the Engine-trait structure from KAS-0) for agent_message_chunk / agent_thought_chunk / tool_call / tool_call_update ONLY.
- session_info_update -> kind:'turn_end' mapped to cyril's turn-completion / busy-clear. THIS — not the session/prompt response — is the turn-completion signal under KAS. HANG-PROOFING: cyril's prompt-response await must be NON-BLOCKING under KAS — drive completion off turn_end, treat the prompt response as secondary/whenever-it-arrives, so a non-returning response cannot freeze the skeleton. Verify with existing probe-kas-* scripts (no new probe needed).
- Answer _kiro/terminal/shell_type ({sessionId} -> {shellType: bash|zsh|fish|powershell|sh}) — lightest host callback, called once at session setup; missing reply yields 'Shell: undefined' in the system prompt.
- Handle sess_*-prefixed session ids and per-run log dirs under ~/.kiro/logs/<ts>/.
- Defensive unknown-variant tolerance for everything else (one debug! line noting _kiro/* surfaces are intentionally dormant pre-2b).

CRITICAL REWORK (ROADMAP note, docs/ROADMAP.md:177): the off-loop busy-guard + TurnCompleted emission from PR #22 (cyril-84ca) must be reworked here. That PR emits TurnCompleted when conn.prompt().await resolves and keys the one-turn busy-guard off prompt_task.is_finished() — both encode 'prompt response == turn end', correct for v2 but FALSE for KAS (turn-end is session_info_update->turn_end; the prompt response is secondary/late). Under KAS, is_finished() stays false after a logical turn-end (next prompt wrongly rejected 'a turn is already in progress') and TurnCompleted fires late/never — the exact hang the non-blocking criterion guards against. Fix: route BOTH the busy-guard and TurnCompleted through the engine's turn-end signal (KAS-0 turn-end detection in the Engine trait), not prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await. Concrete code: the SendPrompt guard + spawned prompt_task in bridge.rs.

REFS: ROADMAP KAS-2a (docs/ROADMAP.md:165); existing experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-*.py.
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine: select KAS, authenticate, send a prompt; agent text + tool calls stream and render, the turn completes, and input becomes live again.
* Turn completion is driven by session_info_update->turn_end, NOT the session/prompt response; the prompt-response await is non-blocking (a non-returning response cannot freeze the skeleton).
* The busy-guard and TurnCompleted emission are routed through the engine turn-end signal (not prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await); a second prompt after a logical turn-end is accepted, not rejected with 'a turn is already in progress'.
* _kiro/terminal/shell_type is answered (no 'Shell: undefined').
* sess_*-prefixed session ids handled; unknown _kiro/* variants produce a single debug! line, no hang.
* v1/v2 engine path unchanged.
notes:
2026-06-21 — The KAS-1 FREE PATH unblocks this demo without cyril-evwh's full auth responder. Direct-spawn `acp-server.js --transport=stdio` with no --auth authenticates from the SSO cache (after `kiro-cli login`): 0 _kiro/auth/getAccessToken calls across init + session/new + a tool turn (probe-kas-direct-spawn-2.8.1.py, 2.8.1). The cyril-evwh "blocks" dependency still holds for the production/wrapper path, but a demo is reachable sooner via the free path — worth weighing in triage.

Skeleton facts captured live (validate the KAS-2a acceptance criteria against these):
  - session/new returns sessionId "sess_..."; _meta.agentMode "vibe"; configOptions POPULATED (mode: vibe/spec/quick-spec/bug-fix/plan/autonomous/semantic_reviewer, autopilot on/off, contentCollection) — unlike v2 where configOptions is null (relevant to KAS-4 / cyril has no issue yet).
  - agentCapabilities._meta.kiro: checkpoints, sessionList, policyNotifications, extensionMethods [_kiro/knowledge, _kiro/codeIntelligence, _kiro/session/{context,compact,export,history}]; sessionCapabilities {fork, list} non-empty.
  - Turn-end rides session_info_update (~10x in one tiny turn); the session/prompt response also returned {stopReason: end_turn}. The NON-BLOCKING acceptance criterion (drive completion off turn_end, treat prompt response as secondary) can be validated on the free path today.
  - No agent->client callbacks on a no-tools turn (this config advertised no host capabilities). _kiro/terminal/shell_type did NOT fire here, and that is EXPECTED, not an open question: shell_type is gated on advertising terminal capability (acp-server.js resolveCapabilities: terminal === true). probe-kas-fs-terminal-host-2.7.1.py advertised terminal:true and saw shell_type fire once at session setup (also docs/kiro-2.7.1-wire-audit.md:344). So when wiring the KAS-2a shell_type responder, advertise terminal capability and answer once at setup; with no terminal cap it correctly never fires.

title: Bridge command loop blocks on conn.prompt() for the whole turn — no mid-turn commands (steer/cancel)
description: prove-it-prototype (cyril-bm1j) proved: bridge.rs awaits conn.prompt().await INSIDE the single-consumer command loop (while let Some(cmd)=command_rx.recv()), so it never recv()s a queued command until the turn ends. Wire oracle: SteerSession enqueued at +1.5s did not cross the wire until 12.839s, 1ms after the session/prompt response at 12.838s. Consequences: (1) mid-turn steering (K1b core) is impossible as-is; (2) STRONGLY IMPLICATED but not separately probed: Esc-while-busy CancelRequest goes through the same loop, so mid-turn cancel is also non-functional today. Fix direction: drive the prompt off the command loop (spawn_local the conn.prompt() future; resolve TurnCompleted from that task) so the loop stays free to process SteerSession/CancelRequest concurrently. Repro: .k1b-steering/probe.rs + oracle-wire-capture.log.
acceptance_criteria: A command enqueued mid-turn is sent to the agent before TurnCompleted (probe: steer surfaces before turn end). Esc-cancel aborts a busy turn promptly. Regression fence: an integration test driving the bridge with a slow turn + a mid-turn command, asserting the command's effect precedes turn completion.
notes: Implementation complete on fix/bridge-loop-midturn-commands (7 slices, checkpointed-build). Off-loop prompt dispatch: SendPrompt spawn_local's the turn on an Rc<conn>; one-in-flight guard via JoinHandle::is_finished; Shutdown aborts. Fences C1(loop frees) C2(steer mid-turn) C3(cancel resolves Cancelled) C4(single turn) C6(one TurnCompleted) C7(shutdown) C9(guard self-clears) all pass via an in-process fake-agent harness; 734 workspace tests + clippy + fmt clean; prove-it oracles (test_bridge, steer/cancel/notif_order probes) agree vs real kiro 2.8.0. C5 (ordering) resolved empirically — holds for v2 AND KAS, not a cyril-84ca property; theoretical zero-gap library race filed cyril-9akh. Ready for PR; unblocks K1b (cyril-bm1j).

title: KAS-4 constraint: never carry effortLevel across a model switch — re-derive from rebuilt configOptions
description:
Design constraint for the KAS-4 configOptions work, from the 2026-07 'Invalid additionalModelRequestFields' investigation. That error is a server-side ValidationException (the phrase appears in no client code) raised when an effort level invalid for the current model reaches the backend inside GenerateAssistantResponseCommand.additionalModelRequestFields.

KAS guards the live-switch path (applyModelId clamps; set_config_option effortLevel validates enum membership) but has three unguarded seams a client can trip: (1) persisted-session restore sets session.effortLevel from metadata with no validation against the current model; (2) session/new _meta.kiro.settings.effortLevel is a bare zod string().optional() — sent through unvalidated; (3) send time pairs a possibly-stale session.effortLevel with a freshly-looked-up effortSchemaPath and only checks both-present, never level-in-enum. Effort enums differ per provider (Anthropic output_config: low..max vs GPT reasoning: different set), so a carried-over level is a live footgun now that GPT models are in the catalog.

When cyril implements KAS configOptions:
- After sending set_config_option {configId: 'model'}, treat the session's effort as unknown until the rebuilt configOptions arrive; re-derive the valid level list (and currentValue) from the effortLevel select option in that response.
- Never send set_config_option {configId: 'effortLevel'} with a cached value from a previous model.
- Never pre-seed _meta.kiro.settings.effortLevel on session/new from a stored value without validating it against the target model's advertised levels (or simply omit it and let KAS pick defaultEffortLevel).
- On loadSession, trust the effortLevel in the returned configOptions, not any cyril-side cache.

See memory reference-kiro-effort-schema-negotiation for the full mechanism and code sites in the KAS bundle.
acceptance_criteria: KAS-4 implementation demonstrably re-derives effort options from the configOptions rebuild after every model switch (test covering switch-then-set-effort ordering); no code path sends an effortLevel value that did not come from the current model's advertised options; session/new omits or validates _meta.kiro.settings.effortLevel.

title: KAS-0 — Engine trait + v2 port
description: Engine abstraction trait + port of current v2 (Rust) behavior behind it. Will later absorb K1a's three new convert/kiro.rs steering arms into the V2Engine impl at negligible cost. First milestone of the KAS engine integration track.
design:
KAS-0 design — synthesized via grill-with-docs (2026-06-21). Decisions D1-D9; full rationale in ADR-0004 + ADR-0001/0002. Acceptance = strict v2 behavioral parity (no visible change).

D1 Turn-end ownership: KAS-0 OWNS the v2 turn-end rewiring (not deferred to KAS-2a) — only way to prove the seam under v2 parity.
D2 Turn-end marker: no predicate/enum. Notification::TurnCompleted is the universal marker both engines converge on (v2 synthesizes from prompt() response; KAS maps session_info_update->turn_end). Busy-state becomes a flag cleared by observing the marker.
D3 Flag location: loop-local turn_in_flight: Option<SessionId>, set on SendPrompt, cleared when the loop observes TurnCompleted. Replaces prompt_task.is_finished() at bridge.rs:395 (busy-guard) + :455 (cancel-target). JoinHandle retained ONLY for Shutdown abort (bridge.rs:1233).
D4 Single-mediator loop (ADR-0004): run_loop select!s over an internal BridgeInbound{Notification,Request} channel. KiroClient + prompt_task feed it; loop forwards to App + clears flag on TurnCompleted. convert STAYS in KiroClient (loop forwards already-converted internal types). Engine shared as Rc<dyn Engine> (KiroClient=convert, loop=client_capabilities at bridge.rs:320).
D5 Request path through loop too, NON-BLOCKING (hard invariant): request_permission builds PermissionRequest with its embedded responder oneshot, sends to loop; loop forwards to App (identity mediation in v2); App replies on the oneshot BYPASSING the loop. Loop never awaits a human decision. Slow KAS-5 resolution spawns off-loop. Captured in ADR-0004.
D6 Capability sub-traits via defaulted accessors on Engine: fn as_auth_responder(&self)->Option<&dyn AuthResponder>{None}. KAS-0 stubs ONE worked example (AuthResponder, KAS-1's) + accessor; V2Engine overrides nothing. Open/closed: new surface = 1 sub-trait + 1 defaulted accessor, v2 untouched.
D7 AgentEngine{V2,Kas} typed selector (--agent-engine flag/config, default v2), NOT argv-sniffed from AgentCommand. run_bridge picks Box<dyn Engine> at spawn: V2=>V2Engine; Kas=>clean "not available yet" via existing error path. No KAS spawn / flag-version resolution (both KAS-1).
D8 kas cargo feature: cyril-core owns kas=[] (empty), cyril forwards kas=["cyril-core/kas"], cyril-ui none. Dedicated "kas-feature" CI job: build+clippy(-D warnings)+test -p cyril --features kas.
D9 Verification spike (parallel, NON-gating): reuse experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-* to capture a live KAS turn's session/update frames -> commit fixtures -> Rust test deserializes each into acp::SessionNotification, asserts Ok (CI-able, no live KAS). A failing variant = documented KAS-2a upgrade-trigger (no #[serde(other)] on SessionUpdate), not a KAS-0 blocker.

V2Engine = thin wrapper delegating to existing convert::session_update_to_notification (mod.rs:393) + convert::kiro::to_ext_notification (kiro.rs:173) — functions don't move.

Suggested slices (each ends green on all v2 tests): (1) Engine trait + V2Engine + client_capabilities-from-engine; (2) single-mediator loop (notifications + requests; flag replaces is_finished); (3) AgentEngine selector + run_bridge gate; (4) kas feature + AuthResponder stub + accessor + CI job; (5) verification spike fixtures+test.

Hazards handed forward — KAS-2a: post-turn_end, the still-running prompt task must not emit a competing late TurnCompleted, and a new SendPrompt must not orphan a live JoinHandle. KAS-5: slow request resolution spawns off-loop, never await in the loop (ADR-0004).
acceptance_criteria: Invisible foundation — ZERO user-visible change. Oracle is behavioral: (a) every existing v2 test passes; (b) a live `kiro-cli acp` session streams text/thoughts, runs tool calls, renders subagents, handles permission approve/cancel, and mode-picks IDENTICALLY to pre-refactor; (c) `--agent-engine kas` cleanly reports "not available yet" (no panic); (d) `kas-feature` CI job (build+clippy+test --features kas) is green; (e) verification-spike deser test passes on captured KAS session/update fixtures. Per-slice: each slice ends with all v2 tests + clippy -D warnings + fmt clean.
notes:
Reconstructed 2026-06-17 from .k1a-steering/related-issues.md; detail from ROADMAP KAS track.

Turn-end coupling (from PR #22 / cyril-84ca, 2026-06-17): the off-loop prompt dispatch made TurnCompleted be emitted when conn.prompt().await resolves, and the one-turn busy-guard keys off prompt_task.is_finished(). Both assume 'prompt response == turn end' — true for v2, FALSE for KAS (KAS-2a: turn-end is session_info_update->turn_end; the prompt response is secondary/late). Under KAS, is_finished() stays false after a logical turn-end, so a legitimate next prompt is wrongly rejected with 'a turn is already in progress', and TurnCompleted fires late/never. When this milestone introduces the Engine trait's turn-end detection and ports v2 behind V2Engine, route BOTH the busy-guard and TurnCompleted emission through the engine's turn-end signal, NOT prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await. Concrete code: bridge.rs SendPrompt guard + the spawned prompt_task.

title: KAS-1: decide cyril's ACP clientInfo.name — it silently selects KAS persona, remote-tool allowlist branch, and hooks injection
description:
Discovered in the 2.13.0 wire audit (docs/kiro-2.13.0-wire-audit.md, memory reference_kiro_2_13_0_diff). KAS derives its entire client identity from the ACP initialize clientInfo.name via resolveAgentContext(env, clientInfo.name, caps). Only 'kiro-web' | 'kiro-ide' | 'kiro-cli' are recognized; ANY other name logs 'Unrecognized clientInfo.name: <x>, falling back to inferred client type' and becomes 'kiro-ide' (local). cyril currently sends its own product name, so on KAS it silently lands in the kiro-ide branch. Three downstream effects, all decided by this one string:

1. Remote-tool allowlist branch (resolveRemoteToolAllowlist): kiro-ide is CHANNEL-gated (stable -> [web_search], insider -> [web_search, searchMemories]); the memoryEnabled gate (searchMemories opt-in) exists ONLY on the kiro-cli branch. So cyril cannot reach the memoryEnabled path unless it presents clientInfo.name='kiro-cli'. (KIRO_LOAD_ALL_REMOTE_TOOLS=true forces '*' as a debug bypass.)
2. System-prompt PERSONA (getIdentity): 'You are Kiro CLI, an agent...' vs 'You are Kiro, an agentic...' (IDE). cyril inherits whichever persona its name maps to.
3. Hooks block is injected into the system prompt ONLY for kiro-ide (client === 'kiro-ide' ? hooksBlock : '').

DECISION NEEDED: what should cyril present as clientInfo.name to KAS? Presenting 'kiro-cli' gets the CLI persona + the memoryEnabled allowlist branch but misrepresents the client (also feeds AWS telemetry per reference_kiro_acp_telemetry, and cyril's real name is the honest value). Presenting cyril's own name is honest but forces the kiro-ide fallback (IDE persona + hooks block + channel-gated tools). This is a KAS-1 handshake knob distinct from cyril-nhzw (_meta.kiro.settings / AgentSettings flags) and cyril-evwh (engine selection + getAccessToken). Not blocking current v2 path. Relates: cyril-nhzw, cyril-evwh, cyril-6iek.


--- TRIAGE 2026-07-18 (hooksBlock verified from the extracted 2.13.0 KAS bundle, acp-server.js) ---

WHAT THE HOOKS BLOCK IS: a <hooks> system-prompt section briefing the MODEL on the
hooks feature: hook files at .kiro/hooks/<id>.json; "use the createHook tool, do NOT
write hook files manually with fs_write"; the full trigger vocabulary (UserPromptSubmit,
Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreTaskExec, PostTaskExec, PostFileSave, PostFileCreate,
PostFileDelete, SessionStart); action types (agent = append prompt to context, command =
shell with JSON on stdin); exit-code semantics (0 = success, stdout forwarded for
SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse; 2 = BLOCK the action, stderr forwarded;
other = silent failure); the hook-file JSON schema; and IDE-only UI pointers
("explorer view 'Agent Hooks'", command palette "Open Kiro Hook UI").

KEY MECHANIC: this gate controls what the model is TOLD, not whether hooks RUN.
Execution rides the _meta.kiro.hooks handshake + host responders (KAS-7, cyril-jiyn).
Briefing keyed on clientInfo.name, machinery keyed on the _meta handshake -> the two
can desync into states Kiro never tested:
- name=cyril (kiro-ide fallback, today's accidental state): model briefed, but with IDE
  affordances that do not exist in a TUI -> wrong instructions to cyril users.
- name=kiro-cli: zero briefing while machinery can be live -> hook-injected context
  unattributed by the model; exit-2 PreToolUse blocks unexplained; "set up a hook"
  requests answered by hand-writing JSON (the exact failure the createHook prose exists
  to prevent).

BIGGER THAN THE AUDIT'S 3 BULLETS: the entire KAS base prompt is client-keyed —
getIdentity / getSessionTypes / getAutonomyModes / getChatContext / getSteering all
take client; hooksBlock is merely the only all-or-nothing section (ternary to "").

RECOMMENDATION: keep clientInfo.name = cyril's own name (honest; clientInfo feeds AWS
telemetry per reference_kiro_acp_telemetry; consistent with the be-legitimate stance
from the pi-kiro analysis). Treat the losses explicitly:
1. IDE persona: cosmetic.
2. memoryEnabled/searchMemories path unreachable: (a) upstream ask — recognize
   third-party ACP clients or key these gates off capabilities/_meta instead of the
   name; (b) before building any presentAs='kiro-cli' escape hatch, probe whether
   KIRO_LOAD_ALL_REMOTE_TOOLS=true already covers the power-user case.
3. Hooks-briefing mismatch: mitigable client-side — cyril injects a corrected hooks
   briefing (steering/context) when hooks are configured; couple with KAS-7.

DECIDE TOGETHER WITH cyril-jiyn (KAS-7); do not settle the name and the hooks
handshake independently.

--- DECISION RATIFIED 2026-07-18 (pause) / implemented on PR #61, pending merge ---
Honest default clientInfo {name:"cyril", title:"Cyril"} + opt-in [agent] present_as = "kiro-cli" knob (KAS-only, inert-with-warn on v2, title never impersonated). Recorded in docs/adr/0006-clientinfo-identity.md; fail-loud startup advisory because the classification is not exposed by the initialize response. The DECISION NEEDED block above is resolved; kept for history.

title: Wire-audit A/B corpus: add steering echo lifecycle so echo renames are caught
description: The v2 steering-echo rename shipped in kiro-cli 2.9.0 and went unnoticed through four release audits because the A/B same-day corpus (experiments/conductor-spike) never exercises _session/steer. Every audit truthfully reported 'v2 wire frozen' for the audited corpus while the steering surface drifted. Add to the audit corpus: idle steer -> clear lifecycle + busy-turn steer (queued/injected/cleared frames, both engines). Probe templates exist: .cyril-vgcm/probe-steer-clear-{live,behavior}-2.12.0.py. title: Route _kiro.dev/metadata by params.sessionId — subagent metadata frames currently stamp the main toolbar
description:
v2 _kiro.dev/metadata frames carry a params-level sessionId, and during agent_crew runs subagent sessions emit their OWN metadata frames: the committed captures prove it (experiments/conductor-spike/trace-2.4.1-multi-subagent.jsonl has metadata frames for 5 distinct sessionIds; trace-2.4.1-tui-recorder.jsonl has 4). Kiro's own TUI filters them — carved 2.12.1 tui.js handleMetadataUpdate begins: let n=e.sessionId; if(n && n!==this.sessionId) return.

Cyril does not: client.rs ext_notification routes everything except ToolCallChunk as RoutedNotification::global, so MetadataUpdated from a subagent session is applied to the main SessionController/UiState. Consequences during any multi-subagent v2 turn: main toolbar context-usage % flickers with subagent percentages, subagent per-turn credits/duration are attributed to the main session, and a subagent effort value could overwrite the main badge.

Fix shape: extract params.sessionId in convert (or promote it in client.rs the way ToolCallChunk already does) and route MetadataUpdated scoped; frames with no sessionId stay global. Decide explicitly what a subagent-scoped metadata frame updates (likely: the subagent stream state or nothing, mirroring Kiro TUI's drop) — and keep main-session behavior byte-identical for single-session turns.
acceptance_criteria: MetadataUpdated from a frame whose sessionId matches a known subagent no longer mutates main-session context_usage/metering/effort; single-session metadata behavior unchanged (existing tests pass); regression test replays a main-metadata + subagent-metadata sequence and asserts main toolbar values come only from the main frame; frames without sessionId still apply to main.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #54 (8d28784). Repro: metadata_routing_tests::subagent_metadata_does_not_stamp_main_session (wire-shaped frames from trace-2.4.1-multi-subagent.jsonl replayed through real ext_notification + App routing contract). Companion fence: metadata_without_session_id_still_applies_to_main. Fix mirrors the ToolCallChunk scoped-promotion idiom; empty/absent sessionId stays global.
