title: Upstream ask: KAS should recognize third-party ACP clients (or key persona/allowlist/hooks off capabilities, not clientInfo.name)
description: DEFERRED FROM the cyril-0wyn ADR. Evidence in .cyril-0wyn/ (probes + carves, KAS 2.13.0): resolveAgentContext recognizes only kiro-web|kiro-ide|kiro-cli; any other clientInfo.name silently becomes kiro-ide (local) — the misclassification is invisible on the wire (initialize responses byte-identical across names, findings.md Q3). At least FOUR behaviors key off the resolved client: system-prompt persona (getIdentity), remote-tool allowlist (resolveRemoteToolAllowlist: memoryEnabled gate is kiro-cli-only), hooksBlock system-prompt injection (kiro-ide-only), and honorsRepositories (kiro-web/sandbox-only). ASK (human action, raising with the Kiro team): recognize third-party ACP clients — e.g. treat unknown names as a generic 'acp-client' with an explicit capability-negotiated feature set, or key persona/allowlist/hooks off KiroClientMeta capabilities instead of the name; at minimum, surface the resolved client type in the initialize response so clients can fail loud. cyril's interest: honest clientInfo.name (see ADR in docs/adr/ from cyril-0wyn) without losing the memoryEnabled path (cyril-jrl1 is the impersonation escape hatch we'd rather not build). Related: cyril-jiyn (hooksBlock coupling).

title: KAS user_input questions: cyril can only return the FIRST option (kind-keyed permission response drops the choice)
description: KAS's user_input clarifying-question flow (spec/quick-spec) surfaces each question three ways at once: (1) tool_call kind:'other' _meta.kiro.toolId:'user_input' (display card — NOTE cyril FILTERS kind==Other tool calls, so this card is suppressed), (2) session_info_update pending_interaction (KAS-native mirror — cyril drops unknown session_info_update kinds), (3) session/request_permission (the id'd REQUEST cyril actually handles via its approval overlay). So the question reaches the user through the permission overlay. BUG: these questions carry N answer options ALL with kind 'allow_once' (e.g. 'A list of discrete note entries' / 'Keep the blob, add structured append' / 'Skip questions'). cyril's PermissionResponse enum is KIND-based (AllowOnce/AllowAlways/Reject/RejectAlways/Cancel), and to_acp_permission_response uses find_option_id(args, kind) which returns the FIRST option matching that kind (convert/mod.rs ~345). With all options kind=allow_once, cyril ALWAYS returns option-0's id regardless of which the user picked -> the agent silently receives the wrong answer to a design-decision question. The overlay knows state.selected (the index) but PermissionResponse can't carry it. Correct wire reply is {outcome:{outcome:'selected', optionId:'...-option-N'}} for the SPECIFIC chosen option. Evidence: experiments/conductor-spike/kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl (3 user_input questions, ids 3/4/5, each 3x allow_once). Only bites on KAS/v3 + spec/quick-spec/user_input flows; silent (wrong answer, no error).
acceptance_criteria: PermissionResponse can carry the selected optionId (e.g. a Selected(PermissionOptionId) variant or index); overlay passes through the chosen option's id; same-kind multi-option requests (user_input) return the exact choice; standard tool approvals (distinct kinds) unchanged. Verify against the trace's 3 questions.
notes:
SCOPE WIDENED (2026-07-02, from the KAS trace's fs_write tool_approval, id=6): the permission-response fix is bigger than 'carry optionId'. KAS tool_approvals carry _meta.kiro.consent on BOTH request and response. Request: {capability:'fs_write', resource, askType:'implicit', workspaceRoot} + consentRound. The reference client's response ECHOES the full outcome shape: {_meta:{kiro:{consent:{capability, scope, workspaceRoot}}}, outcome:{outcome:'selected', optionId:'accept', _meta:{kiro:{consent:{capability, scope, workspaceRoot}}}}} where scope='invocation' for allow_once (presumably session/always for allow_always). cyril's to_acp_permission_response sends NO _meta at all — so an 'Always allow' grant may not record its scope (ties to trust-persistence [reference_kiro_trust_persistence] — the wire consent scope may be the KAS-native mechanism vs cyril writing the agent store file). => The new PermissionResponse must carry: (a) the selected optionId (the original bug), AND (b) an optional _meta.kiro.consent{capability, scope} passthrough for tool_approvals. CAVEAT: whether KAS REQUIRES the consent _meta vs derives scope from the option kind is UNVERIFIED (schema-present ≠ required, feedback_kiro_schema_vs_runtime) — needs a control probe: respond 'accept' WITHOUT consent _meta, check grant/scope still registers. Standard tool approvals (distinct kinds) already answer correctly today; this widening is about grant-SCOPE fidelity + the user_input optionId bug.

TRUST-MODEL DIVERGENCE (2026-07-02, from the tool-lifecycle KAS-vs-v2 diff): the permission-response outcome shape is ENGINE-CONDITIONAL — the two engines' trust wire has ZERO field overlap. v2: permission carries _meta.trustOptions[].{patterns[], setting_key, label, display}; response echoes outcome._meta.trustOption.{patterns[], setting_key} (command-pattern trust the client persists to a setting_key — the allowedCommands mechanism, reference_kiro_trust_persistence). cyril already handles v2 trustOptions (trust_options in PermissionRequest, 7 files). KAS: permission carries _meta.kiro.consent{capability, resource, askType} + consentRound; response echoes outcome._meta.kiro.consent{capability, scope('invocation'|session|always), workspaceRoot} (Cedar capability+scope grant, NOT a command pattern). => the new PermissionResponse can't be a single shape: it needs to carry EITHER a v2 trustOption passthrough OR a KAS consent{capability,scope}, selected by engine. This widens the fix again: (a) selected optionId [orig bug], (b) KAS consent{capability,scope} passthrough for tool_approvals, (c) keep the v2 trustOption path. The converter split (convert/kiro.rs v2 trustOptions vs convert/kas.rs KAS consent) is the right seam.

PROBE COMPLETE (2026-07-02, prove-it-prototype): probe = crates/cyril-core/src/protocol/convert/probe_qo13.rs (cargo test -p cyril-core probe_qo13 -- --nocapture) replays all 10 trace permission requests through the REAL parse+convert pipeline; oracle = .cyril-qo13/oracle.py (raw-text extraction of reference-client replies, no cyril code). AGREE item-by-item on all 10 requests x every option: correct reply = options[k].optionId; cyril-current emits option-0 for EVERY pick on all-allow_once requests (11/16 pick-cases wrong on ids 1-5,10) while all 16 distinct-kind fs_write cases are correct. CAVEAT RESOLVED for allow_once: the reference client itself omitted consent _meta on requests 8/9 (present on 6/7, all consentRound=1) and all four fs_writes completed -> consent echo OPTIONAL for invocation-scope grants; no control probe needed. Also: user_input replies are BARE selected outcomes (no _meta at either level), and outcome:'cancelled' is legal (req 1 cancelled -> KAS re-asked as req 2), so Esc->Cancel already wire-valid. RESIDUAL UNKNOWN: whether allow_always grants need the consent _meta scope echo to persist (trace has no always-accept reply) - live control probe needed only for the consent-passthrough half. Full findings: .cyril-qo13/probe-findings.md

DESIGN READY (2026-07-02, falsifiable-design): .cyril-qo13/design.md. Core fix: PermissionResponse -> Selected{option_id: PermissionOptionId, trust_option} | Cancel; ApprovalPhase::SelectTrust carries the phase-1 chosen_option_id (selected re-indexes trust_options in phase 2 -- the hidden hazard); find_option_id + fallbacks deleted. 8 claims, cheapest falsifier PASSED (reply encoding byte-equal to reference client's non-first-option reply -- probe_qo13_reply_shape_matches_reference_bytes). Unknown option kinds proven UNREACHABLE (acp 0.10.2 parse rejects; sentinel test + watch item cyril-p7kp). SCOPE SPLIT per design: KAS allow_always consent-scope -> cyril-gn07; v2 trustOption echo shape -> cyril-sive; this issue = exact-choice fidelity only. Next: budgeted-plan.

PLAN READY (2026-07-02, budgeted-plan): .cyril-qo13/plan.md — 6 slices, expand/contract (workspace green after every slice): 1 PermissionOptionId newtype (mechanical) -> 2 expand Selected variant + converter arm + load-bearing membership warn -> 3 replay probe upgraded to asserting C1/C3 fence (both traces + synthetic single-option) -> 4 UI phase-1 exact pick + oob/empty->Cancel -> 5 SelectTrust{chosen_option_id} provenance (+staleness fixture for back-then-repick) -> 6 contract: delete kind variants/From impl/find_option_id, fix bridge harness. Post-slice: one-time live KAS validation of C8 (user-approved manual fence). Next: checkpointed-build.

BUILD COMPLETE (2026-07-02, checkpointed-build): 6/6 slices landed on main (88988f2, 2ba2fde, 5bcca62, 758f411, ac30697, be95aef). All fences green: exact-choice replay over BOTH 2.11.0 traces + synthetic single-option (C1/C3), reply-shape byte-equality vs reference (C2), trust-provenance + staleness fixtures (C4), cancel/oob/empty (C5/C6), unknown-kind sentinel (C7). Mutation checks proved fences non-vacuous (forced option-0 fails 4 tests; options[selected]-at-trust-confirm fails provenance). 798 tests default / 872 --features kas, clippy --all-targets clean both. C8 MANUAL FENCE: cyril-side = deterministic (C1+C2); server-side LIVE: KAS 2.11.0 accepted cyril's exact bytes for a NON-FIRST option ('always-accept') and honored it (probe experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-user-input-choice-2.11.0.py, after auth workaround — see cyril-dcc6). The user_input-question live re-run (non-first pick on the mode question) still PENDING a fresh token window: the empty-tempdir session explored instead of entering the spec flow, and the IDC token expired before a retry; user_input semantic honoring stands proven by the committed reference trace (ids 2/3/10, non-first picks steering the spec). Rerun when logged in fresh: python3 experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-user-input-choice-2.11.0.py <acp-server.js path>.

Closed: Build complete on main: 6/6 slices (88988f2..be95aef) + review commit 0878f2c (independent C3 oracle wired into replay fence). Acceptance criteria verified: exact-choice replay over both 2.11.0 traces + synthetic single-option (C1/C3), reply-shape byte-equality vs reference client (C2), trust-provenance/staleness (C4), cancel/oob/empty (C5/C6), unknown-kind sentinel (C7); mutation checks proved fences non-vacuous. C8 live: KAS 2.11.0 accepted+honored a non-first-option reply (tool_approval always-accept); user_input semantic honoring proven by committed reference trace (ids 2/3/10). RESIDUAL (optional, non-blocking): live user_input non-first-pick re-run pending fresh IDC token — probe-kas-user-input-choice-2.11.0.py. Scope splits live on: cyril-gn07 (KAS allow_always consent scope), cyril-sive (v2 trustOption echo).

C8 LIVE COMPLETE (2026-07-03, fresh IDC token window): the pending user_input live re-run PASSED. Probe v2 (rewritten in place: probe-kas-user-input-choice-2.11.0.py) reproduces the reference trace's recipe — throwaway local clone of ~/repos/rivets as workspace, set_config_option autopilot=on + mode=spec + model=claude-haiku-4.5, then the trace's spec prompt. A genuine user_input question fired ('Is this a new feature or a bugfix?', 3 options all allow_once, optionIds <toolCallId>-option-N). cyril's exact reply bytes for options[1] ('Fix a Bug') were accepted AND semantically honored: the agent continued 'Got it—this is a bugfix' and branched into the bug-detail flow instead of option-0's feature path. v1 lesson encoded in the probe: user_input never fires from a bare prompt in an empty dir — it is a spec-mode behavior, and permission classification must distinguish standard tool approvals (accept/always-accept/... optionIds) from question sets (all kind=allow_once, non-standard optionIds). Auth via the cyril-dcc6 workaround, refined: file seeded from the sqlite IDC token WITHOUT refreshToken sufficed for the whole session; original file restored after. NO RESIDUAL REMAINS on this issue.

title: Make theme-dependent cache identities structurally complete
description: Review finding #7: highlight and Markdown cache keys manually enumerate all theme roles, allowing future fields to compile without participating and causing over-broad invalidation. Introduce dependency-appropriate structural cache identities.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Adding a relevant theme field cannot silently bypass cache identity.
- [ ] Highlight cache identity includes only rendering dependencies.
- [ ] Cross-theme cache isolation tests pass.

title: Bridge: offer agent respawn/reconnect after BridgeDisconnected (post-l7tw)
description: cyril-l7tw makes engine death visible (BridgeError on the failed turn + BridgeDisconnected with stderr-tail detail, run_loop exits). Deliberately OUT of l7tw scope: recovering without restarting cyril. Candidate shape: a /reconnect command (or automatic prompt-on-disconnect) that respawns the agent process and re-runs the handshake with a fresh session, preserving UI history. Needs: App-side re-create of BridgeHandle (spawn_bridge is one-shot today), UiState reset semantics on reconnect (SessionCreated vs a new ReconnectedNotification), and care with in-flight permission responders. Related: cyril-cx27 (SessionController context reset on disconnect).

title: dispatch_steer: optimistic chip+echo leak if bridge.send fails after add_steer_echo
description: app.rs dispatch_steer SteerGate::Send arm calls ui.add_steer_echo(&text) (chip +1, Queued echo committed) BEFORE bridge.send(BridgeCommand::SteerSession).await?. If the channel send errors (bridge thread gone / channel closed), the ? propagates and the function unwinds, but the chip increment and the Queued echo are already committed with no drain path — the steer never reached the backend so no SteeringConsumed/Cleared/Unsupported will arrive. With cyril-7z7u removing the turn-end reset, the chip sticks until /new. Low severity (trigger is a dead bridge, steering already broken) but a real unreconcilable optimistic-UI leak. Fix: either send first and add the echo only on Ok, or roll back (decrement chip + flip the just-added echo to a terminal status) in the Err arm before returning. Found by /code-review of PR 28 (finding #2). app.rs:966.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #40 (bd837e7). Repro: failed_steer_send_leaves_no_phantom_chip.

title: KAS-3 — Subagent rendering must handle dropped/empty/never-terminal results (upstream Kiro #9542)
description:
KAS subagent results can legitimately be empty, dropped, or never delivered — cyril's KAS-3 agent-subtask rendering (ROADMAP KAS-3; unfiled until now) must degrade gracefully. Surfaced by upstream Kiro bug #9542 (subagent "No result").

THREE upstream failure modes cyril will observe on the wire:
1. (v2) premature channel close: parent goes Idle before the subagent's `summary` is delivered -> stage result = "No result" (trace WARN: 'tool execution event for an agent not processing tools ... active_state: Idle, executing_subagents: {}').
2. (v3/KAS) null-response schema-validation fail: subagent hits max_tokens on long output -> response=null -> "No result". Intermittent, output-length-driven.
3. session-level channel corruption: a CANCELLED multi-stage `depends_on` pipeline leaves the multiplexer channel un-reset -> ALL subsequent subagent calls return "No result" until CLI restart.

cyril impact (cyril is an OBSERVER — can't fix upstream, must render robustly):
- Crew panel / SubagentUiState must NOT show a subagent as perpetually 'running' when its result is dropped/never-terminal. Treat a quiet subagent with no terminal result as a terminal 'no result / interrupted' state. On KAS the "No result" arrives as the parent's `orchestrate_subagent` tool_call_update content — render faithfully.
- HAZARD cyril can TRIGGER: Esc -> session/cancel during a KAS multi-stage subagent pipeline is exactly the Ctrl+C path that triggers upstream mode 3 (session-wide channel corruption). cyril should guard/warn, and if subagent results start coming back empty, surface a 'subagent channel may be wedged — /new to recover' hint.
- Repro/verify with the ACP wire recorder KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH=/tmp/acp-trace.jsonl ({ts,dir,msg} JSONL; lives in tui.js, confirmed 2.8.1).

Engine note: failure mode differs v2 vs v3. Related: cyril-9akh (notification-vs-response ordering race this validates), cyril-j16p (KAS-2a walking skeleton this builds on).
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — split acceptance criteria BY ENGINE (current wording conflates v2 and KAS paths):
- The 'Crew panel / SubagentUiState must not show perpetually running' line applies ONLY to v2 failure mode #1. On KAS those components receive nothing (no list_update); feeding KAS subtask data into SubagentTracker would violate the CLAUDE.md separate-path rule.
- KAS modes #2/#3 belong to the greenfield KAS-3 subtask path: a distinct grouper keyed on _meta.kiro.kind == 'agent-subtask' / agentSubtaskId, with _meta parsing in convert/kas.rs producing engine-neutral typed fields (UiState never reads raw _meta). Nothing in the crates handles agent-subtask yet — KAS-3 is greenfield, right moment to pin this.
- The never-terminal inference (quiet subagent with no terminal result -> render interrupted) and the wedge heuristic (empty results -> hint /new) are stateful inferences that live in that KAS-3 component, NOT SubagentTracker.
- The orchestrate_subagent tool_call_update rendering approach and the App-altitude Esc-hazard guard are architecturally clean as filed.

title: Scale conversation Markdown rendering with the visible viewport
description: Review finding #12: every frame re-renders all historical messages and deep-clones cached Markdown lines before viewport clipping. Redesign layout/rendering so steady-state cost scales with visible content.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A production-shape long-history benchmark exists.
- [ ] Steady-state render work is bounded by the visible window plus documented overscan.
- [ ] Scrolling output remains correct.

title: spawn_bridge parameter clump: bundle engine/kas_spawn/present_as into a spawn-config struct
description: From the cyril-0wyn pre-PR standards review (2026-07-19): spawn_bridge now takes (agent_command, agent_engine, kas_spawn, present_as, cwd) — engine/kas_spawn/present_as already travel together as AgentConfig fields, and adding present_as forced a mechanical edit of 8 call sites (main.rs, test_bridge + l7tw examples, 4 KAS smoke tests, 1 in-file test). Each future [agent] knob repeats the ritual. Fix: a BridgeConfig (or pass AgentConfig) bundling the clump; one signature, call sites shrink. Pure refactor, no behavior change; coordinate with cyril-ytuk if batched.

title: Streamed agent text can render after TurnCompleted (ACP notification-vs-response ordering race)
description: agent-client-protocol (rpc.rs) resolves incoming RESPONSES inline in the read loop (oneshot send) but routes incoming NOTIFICATIONS through a separate incoming_tx queue handled by another task. So when a session/update notification immediately precedes the prompt response on the wire (zero scheduling gap), the response (-> TurnCompleted) can overtake the not-yet-handled notification on cyril's notification channel, committing TurnCompleted before the final streamed AgentMessage. Discovered during cyril-84ca Slice 7 via a zero-gap unit harness. NOT introduced by cyril-84ca (the pre-fix inline conn.prompt().await shares the identical race). Empirically does NOT manifest against real kiro 2.8.0 v2 OR KAS (.cyril-84ca/notif_order.rs: order is [msg,msg,TURN] for v2, [msg,TURN] for KAS) because real backends leave enough timing gap for the notification queue to drain. Low priority: theoretical/edge race, no observed production impact. Possible fixes if it ever surfaces: have the turn task yield/drain pending notifications before emitting TurnCompleted, or coalesce turn-end behind the notification queue.
acceptance_criteria: A unit/integration test reproduces the reorder deterministically AND a fix makes streamed AgentMessages always precede TurnCompleted on the notification channel even at zero wire-gap; verified to still hold against real v2 + KAS.
notes:
2026-06-21 — Real-world corroboration: upstream Kiro bug #9542 (https://github.com/kirodotdev/Kiro/issues/9542) shows the parent agent going Idle while a result is still in flight (trace WARN: 'received a tool execution event for an agent not processing tools ... active_state: Idle, executing_subagents: {}'), dropping the subagent's late result — the same parent-races-its-own-late-result shape as this race, observed in production. Caveat: that bug is the parent<->subagent channel INSIDE kiro (upstream of cyril's ACP wire), so it doesn't directly hit cyril's notification channel, but it proves late/dropped results around turn-end are not purely theoretical. Better repro tool than the synthetic zero-gap harness: KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH=/tmp/acp-trace.jsonl captures full ACP wire traffic as {ts,dir,msg} JSONL (tui.js, confirmed 2.8.1) — use it to capture real notification-vs-response ordering at near-zero gap. Verify any fix on BOTH v2 and v3/KAS (upstream failure mode is engine-specific). Subagent-rendering hazards from #9542 tracked in cyril-fjfu (KAS-3).

2026-07-02 — Ordering ground-truth from a real KAS session (KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH capture, exactly the tool this note recommended). Redacted snapshot committed at experiments/conductor-spike/kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl (527 frames/880s, 2 turns: id=5 cancelled, id=8 completed). On BOTH completed turn boundaries the ONLY session/update trailing turn_end within 5s is a single session_info_update::context_usage (~10ms after the prompt response); NO agent_message_chunk or tool_call arrives after turn_end on either turn. So in this KAS/v3 session the streamed-text-after-TurnCompleted race did NOT manifest on the wire — only benign context telemetry trails turn_end (matches the 'context_usage trails turn_end' note in kas.rs). CAVEAT: v3/KAS only, 2 turns (one cancelled) = one sample, not proof; still verify any fix on BOTH v2 and v3. This is evidence the race is timing-window-narrow at the source, consistent with it being a cyril-side reordering risk rather than a routine backend emission.

title: Engine identity is trusted, never verified — fingerprint the wire at handshake; fail-loud KAS detection must live outside the kas feature
description:
From the 2026-07-02 v2/v3 differentiation-strategy audit. AgentEngine is bound at spawn purely from the flag/config (bridge.rs engine_for); nothing verifies the subprocess actually speaks the assumed dialect, though cheap fingerprints exist: KAS session ids are sess_-prefixed and KAS initialize returns agentCapabilities._meta.kiro (checkpoints/sessionList/extensionMethods); v2 has neither.

The failure mode is ASYMMETRIC because of the kas cargo feature (ADR-0002): a --features kas build meeting v2 degrades gracefully (v2 never calls the responders), but a DEFAULT build meeting KAS breaks with zero diagnostic — V2Engine drops session_info_update turn_end, and _kiro/auth/getAccessToken has no responder compiled in (wrapper spawn), so turns die/hang with nothing actionable. Two realistic triggers: kiro-cli flips its default engine to KAS (clearly the direction), or the engine-flag vocabulary shifts again (kas->v3 already happened at 2.8.0). Related nit: AgentEngine::FromStr rejects 'v3' — kiro-cli's own vocabulary since 2.8.0 is v1/v2/v3, so users will type v3; accept it as an alias for Kas.

Sketch: (a) post-initialize/session-new fingerprint assertion (sess_ prefix, _meta.kiro caps) vs the bound engine; mismatch = loud system message + safe stop, not a debug! line; (b) the DETECTION half compiles unconditionally (outside the kas feature) so a default build can say 'agent is KAS — rebuild with --features kas or pass --agent-engine v2'; (c) FromStr v3 alias. Evidence: audit conversation 2026-07-02; docs/kiro-2.7.1-wire-audit.md (KAS handshake shape); reference client traces experiments/conductor-spike/kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl.
acceptance_criteria: A default (no-kas-feature) cyril run against a KAS-speaking subprocess produces an actionable error message, not a hang or silent dead turn; a --features kas build with the wrong --agent-engine detects the mismatch at handshake and says so; '--agent-engine v3' parses as Kas; unit tests cover the fingerprint check both directions and the FromStr alias.
notes: Closed: Shipped: PR #50 merged to main (4ee37b1). All four ACs fenced: default-build-vs-KAS actionable BridgeDisconnected (fingerprint_stops_v2_bound_on_kas_wire + live POST harness, F4 cascade eliminated); kas-build mismatch detection both directions (unit matrix + kas-lane bridge test); --agent-engine v3 parses as Kas in FromStr AND serde (D7 reversal pause-approved, serialization stays kas); fingerprint unit tests both directions + alias. Two-layer verification: initialize _meta.kiro-object check + sess_ session-id check at session/new (post-response) and session/load (pre-flight), compiled unconditionally outside the kas feature. Fixed in-branch: notify_fingerprint_stop dedup (review finding). Filed: cyril-wkt8 (acp frame-split watch).

title: Optimistic steer chip can stick >0 when a turn-tail steer never drains (no next turn / non-EndTurn / backend drop)
description: cyril-7z7u removed the unconditional TurnCompleted reset of UiState.steering_queued so a steer DEFERRED by the backend to the next turn keeps its chip across turn-end (correct per probe). Residual: the optimistic chip now drains ONLY via SteeringConsumed/Cleared/Unsupported/SessionCreated. If a steer is queued at a turn's tail and NO draining event ever arrives, the chip shows a phantom count until /new. Reachable paths: (a) user steers as the turn ends, then sends no further prompt (no next turn -> no deferred SteeringConsumed); (b) turn ends non-EndTurn / cancelled / errored with the steer un-consumed; (c) backend accepts the steer (Ok queued:true, bridge emits nothing) but never streams a consume. The old code masked all of these by resetting every turn-end. Decide a BOUNDED safety net that does not regress the deferred-steer case (e.g. reset on Activity::Idle rather than Ready, or on the 2nd consecutive TurnCompleted with no intervening SteeringConsumed) — needs the .cyril-7z7u probe to confirm whether consume is guaranteed-eventual. Found by /code-review of PR 28 (finding #1). state.rs:418 TurnCompleted arm.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) The Activity::Idle option is a near-no-op — Idle is set only at startup and on BridgeDisconnected (state.rs:475 / app.rs:166); TurnCompleted sets Ready (state.rs:462), so an Idle-keyed reset never drains the chip during a live session. Rule it out.
(2) Prefer the 2-consecutive-TurnCompleted-without-SteeringConsumed rule: heuristic but CONTRACT-BACKED by the .cyril-7z7u probe (a tail steer's queued+consumed pair defers to exactly the next turn, so two turn-ends without a consume means the steer is provably gone) — still gated on the probe confirming consume-is-guaranteed-eventual, as the issue says.
(3) Path (a) is TRUTHFUL, not phantom: per the probe the steer really is still queued backend-side through an idle tail and will consume when a next turn starts. A chip persisting through an idle tail is correct — scope the safety net to (b)/(c), leave (a) alone.
(4) NEW path (d): BridgeDisconnected (state.rs:465-476) clears context/tokens/activity but NOT steering_queued — a dead bridge leaves the phantom chip too. Make disconnect an explicit drain point of whatever net is chosen.

CALCULUS CHANGE (cyril-vgcm, 2026-07-14): the steering echo pipeline is live again on both engines — queued/consumed/cleared reconcile chips id-scoped now, so the phantom-chip window shrinks to genuine backend drops / no-next-turn cases. Cleared is NO LONGER a full drain point when ids are present (id-scoped, one chip per named id; empty ids = drain-all preserved). Re-evaluate this issue's bounded safety net against the new reconciliation before building it.

title: Keep chat and input usable at 60×16
description:
## What to build

Make layout allocation respond independently to available width and height. At the supported 60×16 floor, the message input must remain operable, chat must retain useful context, autocomplete must not consume permanent rows, and modal overlays must not hide the input.

## Blocked by

- cyril-cc5e
- cyril-ghuu
- cyril-nrnq
- cyril-dij8
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The frame keeps at least three chat rows while a large multiline draft scrolls inside its allocated input area.
- [ ] Autocomplete anchors above the input when vertical space is constrained and remains keyboard operable.
- [ ] Centered modals clamp safely and protect the input; approval, picker, and browse-mode chat remain usable at 60×16.
- [ ] Representative constrained-size tests and the full workspace quality gate pass.
notes: Closed: Shipped: PR #58 merged to main (bd45535). All 4 ACs fenced: chat floor + scrolling draft (layout_floors_hold_across_adversarial_matrix, input_cursor_always_visible, input_scroll_window_matches_oracle); autocomplete above-input + keyboard operable (suggestions_overlay_under_pressure, suggestion_selection_always_visible, floated_autocomplete_is_keyboard_operable); modals never cover input + clamped selection visible + browse mode (modals_never_cover_input, modal_selection fences, browse_mode_usable_at_floor); no-fallback sweep to 1x1; full workspace gates green on 3 platforms. Fixed in-branch: 7 pre-PR review findings. Filed: cyril-2mfa (git-only @-completion), cyril-tymt (window/test-helper consolidation incl. gemini indexing style).

title: KAS layer idiomatic cleanup batch (2026-07-01 implementation review)
description:
Small idiomatic/silent-failure items from the 2026-07-01 implementation review, batched. Each is independently landable; none changes behavior on the happy path.

1. thiserror migration: version.rs returns Result<_, String> (:10,34,47,72); discovery.rs KasMissing has structured variants but implements neither Display nor Error (hand-rolled reason(), flattened at bridge.rs:303 via .map_err(|m| m.reason())); auth.rs internal plumbing is Result<_, String> (:51,137). CLAUDE.md mandates thiserror with structured metadata; string errors cannot be pattern-matched — this bites when cyril-taba must distinguish stale/missing/corrupt token programmatically. KasMissing is 90% there: #[error(...)] attributes would delete reason() outright.
2. auth.rs:152-166 — log (debug!/warn!) before returning Err to KAS on every error path (missing home, unreadable token, missing profileArn, stale). Sibling modules log per the surface-do-not-swallow rule (host_io.rs:94, terminal_io.rs:304-320); auth failures present as opaque dead turns with nothing in cyril.log.
3. convert/kas.rs:43-45 — log before returning None when a context_usage frame lacks usagePercentage; the same function already warns for present-but-unparseable breakdown (:56). Wire drift here would silently freeze the context % display.
4. discovery.rs:111 — NoHome reason is misleading when KIRO_KAS_SERVER_PATH is set (the bundle WAS located via the override; the reason says to set the var that is already set). Add token-path context to that arm; the test matrix (:266) covers no-home only without overrides.
5. client.rs:280 — debug!-log the method name of unhandled _kiro/* ext requests before returning the null default; @kiro/agent adds host callbacks nearly every release and a new required callback currently degrades invisibly.
6. Named constants for the JSON-RPC codes -32602/-32603/-32000 (raw literals in ~12 places across auth.rs, host_io.rs, terminal_io.rs, client.rs).
7. settings.rs:103-172 — rename the minified-JS identifiers (e, n, c, k, ts) carried over from the zme() transcription; replicate semantics, not names.
8. terminal_io.rs:287 — one-line comment on the `code as u32` sign-loss cast (Windows negative NTSTATUS wraps to the conventional unsigned form — the reasoning is sound but appears nowhere).
9. version.rs:19-22 — drop the dead .unwrap_or("") on the infallible first split().next(); the .filter(!is_empty) does the real work.
10. bridge.rs:825-838 — QueryCommandOptions RPC failure collapses to CommandOptionsReceived { options: [] }, indistinguishable from a command with genuinely no options (a PARSE error correctly emits BridgeError). Emit BridgeError for the RPC-failure case too.
11. client.rs:130-140 (pre-existing) — on ext-notification params parse failure, early-return after the error! instead of substituting Value::Null and double-logging through the engine path.
12. settings.rs:63-66 — debug! on the no-home empty-settings fallback (the module's only unlogged fallback; its own doc warns this is exactly the bare-fallback-flags failure shape). Also fix the doc: initialize is once per BRIDGE, not once per session.
13. Document (or make async) the two remaining sync call sites — settings.rs:26 std::fs via KasEngine::client_capabilities (engine.rs:107) and version.rs:48-51 std::process — both provably startup-only, but the layer's own invariant (host_io.rs:15-17) says never call sync fs/process on this runtime, and neither site documents why blocking is acceptable there.

title: Model the full _kiro.dev/metadata surface; open up EffortLevel for backend-defined levels
description:
Effort levels are NOT a closed wire set — they are schema-negotiated per model. The backend's ListAvailableModels returns additionalModelRequestFieldsSchema per model; Anthropic models advertise effort under output_config (low/medium/high/xhigh/max), the newly added GPT models (e.g. gpt-5.6-terra, in the catalog as of 2026-07-14) under reasoning with a DIFFERENT enum. This invalidates the 'closed set on the wire' doc comment on EffortLevel (crates/cyril-core/src/types/session.rs:23): a GPT-style level reaching EffortLevel::from_wire drops to None with a debug log and the toolbar badge silently vanishes.

Two parts:
1. Open up EffortLevel: keep the known variants for typed use, add a fallback that preserves and displays the raw wire string (e.g. Other(String)) instead of dropping unknown levels. Update the doc comment — the set is per-model, backend-defined.
2. Model everything on _kiro.dev/metadata so additions are visible. Currently parsed in convert/kiro.rs: contextUsagePercentage, credits/turnDurationMs (metering), inputTokens/outputTokens/cachedTokens, effort. Anything else present on the wire should either be parsed into the notification or logged at debug when an unrecognized top-level key appears — the point is to KNOW a new field landed (backend rollouts add fields with zero binary change; the metering fields and the 2.12.1 refusal object both arrived this way). The refusal object itself stays with cyril-h8zb (related).

--- Authoritative field inventory (2026-07-14, carved 2.12.1 tui.js handleMetadataUpdate + live captures) ---

v2 _kiro.dev/metadata carries exactly, per the frontend consumer:
1. sessionId — routing guard (Kiro TUI drops frames whose sessionId != current session). Cyril routing gap filed separately.
2. contextUsagePercentage — parsed.
3. meteringUsage[] {value, unit, unitPlural} — cyril sums value; unit/unitPlural dropped (aggregate is fine, but note the per-entry shape).
4. turnDurationMs — parsed.
5. effort — parsed, BUT presence-sensitive on the wire: tui.js checks `"effort" in e` and an explicit `effort: null` is a badge-CLEAR signal. Cyril's convert warns 'present but not a string' and ignores it, and UiState effort is sticky — so an engine-initiated clear is dropped. Mitigated partly by cyril clearing effort on model change; still model the tri-state (absent=no-change / null=clear / string=set).
6. refusal {category, explanation, recommendedModel} — dropped; stays with cyril-h8zb (related).
7. stopReason (e.g. 'CONTENT_FILTERED') — dropped on this path; cyril-h8zb.

NOT on v2 metadata (do not add here): the token/status/usedTools cluster (totalTokens, uncachedInputTokens|inputTokens, outputTokens, cacheReadInputTokens|cachedTokens, cacheWriteInputTokens, status, usedTools, promptTurnSummaries, elapsedTime) is KAS-only — it rides session_info_update kind='turn_completion', which convert/kas.rs currently drops by design; that belongs to KAS-2b scope. Cyril's existing inputTokens/outputTokens/cachedTokens parse on the v2 metadata path has never been observed in committed captures (those names are the LEGACY fallbacks in the KAS normalizer) — keep it, but treat as unconfirmed-on-wire.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown effort level strings render in the toolbar instead of disappearing; EffortLevel doc no longer claims a closed wire set; every currently-known _kiro.dev/metadata field is either parsed or explicitly ignored with a comment; an unrecognized top-level metadata key produces a debug log naming the key; existing metadata tests still pass plus new coverage for the open-enum path and unknown-key logging.

title: KAS-5b: drain terminal pipes at create (reader task) if KAS ever delays wait_for_exit
description: KAS-5b (cyril-ufie) uses the 'wait does the draining' lifecycle: create spawns the child with piped stdout/stderr and returns the id; wait_for_exit moves the child out and calls child.wait_with_output().await, which drains both pipes concurrently with waiting. Safe because the 2.10.0 capture shows KAS calls wait_for_exit IMMEDIATELY after create (consecutive), so the undrained-pipe window is sub-millisecond. RISK: if a future KAS release delays wait_for_exit while the command is chatty (>64KB before wait arrives), the OS pipe buffer fills and the command blocks on write -> stall. Trigger to act: a capture showing KAS create-then-not-immediately-wait, OR a >64KB-output command stalling. Fix: drain-at-create reader task (spawn_local) that streams stdout+stderr into the entry buffer and reaps on exit (also enables true output-so-far + helps cyril-3lh8 reaping). Re-probe before assuming Option B is permanent.
notes: 2026-07-01 issue-set review — the gate is WRONG-BY-OMISSION: 'trigger to act: a future KAS release delaying wait' ignores that cyril-3lh8's cancel path already produces create-without-wait TODAY. A session/cancel landing between create and wait_for_exit means wait never comes; a command that has already written >64KB blocks forever on the full OS pipe (terminal_io.rs:85-104 pipes both streams with no reader until wait, :139) — a PERMANENT orphan plus a registry entry that never evicts. The two issues are mutually invalidating: 3lh8's 'orphan runs until it exits naturally' assumes drained pipes; this issue's 'sub-ms undrained window' assumes wait always arrives. Fix jointly with cyril-3lh8 (session-keyed registry + kill-on-cancel + drain-at-create reader); .kill_on_drop(true) at create is a cheap backstop either way. Keep P4 only if 3lh8's fix kills pre-wait children; otherwise this is effectively P3.

title: KAS-8 — Surface _kiro/safety/* Infrastructure Safety gate (can block tool calls)
description:
KAS-8 functional gap. `_kiro/safety/{statusChanged,propertiesChanged}` — Infrastructure Safety gate, added in kiro-cli 2.8.0 (embedded @kiro/agent). Cedar/AWS-governance signals that CAN BLOCK A TOOL CALL.

Why this matters more than the other notification gaps: it is ENFORCEMENT, not display. cyril's KAS-2a unknown-variant debug! arm silently drops these today, which means cyril can present a tool as runnable that KAS will refuse, or miss a posture change mid-session. Every other dropped notification just loses information; this one desyncs cyril from what KAS will actually permit.

SCOPE:
- Add a converter arm for `_kiro/safety/statusChanged` + `_kiro/safety/propertiesChanged` (pairs with the KAS-2c GovernanceSource sub-trait — same Cedar/governance family).
- Reflect a safety-blocked tool call in the UI (don't render it as a plain pending/allowed tool).
- Surface the safety status/posture so the user understands why a tool was refused.

REFS: covenant capabilities/safety/*.d.ts (on-disk @kiro/agent 0.3.257; NOT yet in the curated docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md catalog — added after 0.3.224); ROADMAP KAS-8 coverage matrix; memory reference_kiro_2_8_0_diff (notes the safety gate). Probe the live shapes before implementing (no probe exists yet).

title: KAS on kiro 2.11.0: bundle discovery misses versioned dirs; prompt errors instantly even with KIRO_KAS_SERVER_PATH
description: Found 2026-07-02 during cyril-qo13 final integration (C8 live check). Two layers: (1) DISCOVERY: cyril-core kas/discovery.rs expects ~/.local/share/kiro-cli/kas/node_modules/@kiro/agent/dist/server/acp-server.js, but kiro >=2.10.0 self-extracts to VERSIONED dirs (kas/<ver>-<sha>/node_modules/...) — both 2.10.0 and 2.11.0 dirs present on this machine, so default discovery fails with 'KAS bundle not found'. Discovery should glob the versioned dirs and pick the one matching the kiro-cli version (or newest). (2) FAST-ERROR TURN: with KIRO_KAS_SERVER_PATH pointed at the 2.11.0 bundle, kas_fs_host_io_smoke -- --ignored dies ~1.7s after SendPrompt with TurnCompleted and zero agent activity (no permission request, no fs callbacks). Token file ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json is VALID (checked expiry). Bisect: identical failure at pre-qo13 commit ac30697 => environment/upstream, not the qo13 slices. Prime suspect: @kiro/agent 0.3.299->0.8.0 RENUMBER in 2.11.0 (reference_kiro_2_11_0_diff) changing a handshake/contract cyril's KAS client relies on — needs a KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH-style capture of cyril-vs-KAS to see the actual error frame. NOTE: all kas_*_smoke tests are #[ignore] so CI never catches this class; consider a cheap 'bundle discoverable' non-ignored check.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND (2026-07-02, qo13 C8 probe): the 'fast-error turn' = backend HTTP 403 'bearer token invalid'. ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json holds a DEAD GitHub-social identity (authMethod:social, provider:Github) whose expiresAt looks fresh but whose bearer the backend rejects; the LIVE credential is the CLI's IDC token in data.sqlite3 auth_kv 'kirocli:odic:token' (+ profileArn in state 'api.codewhisperer.profile'). Standalone-spawned KAS logs 'Auth: default token file' and NEVER calls _kiro/auth/getAccessToken — file-auth-only in this mode; with the file absent it errors 'No auth token found' (no callback fallback). Writing the sqlite IDC token into the file shape {accessToken,refreshToken,profileArn,expiresAt} made KAS 2.11.0 fully functional (real turn, tool approvals honored). => cyril impact: cyril's KAS auth path reads the same file (kas/discovery.rs), so cyril inherits the dead-identity failure; fix should source auth from the CLI sqlite store (or refresh/validate the file) in addition to fixing versioned-dir discovery. CAUTION: KAS self-refreshes the file token — seeding it with the CLI's refresh_token risks consuming a single-use refresh and invalidating the CLI login; any fix must not share the refresh token family carelessly.

WORKAROUND REFINED (2026-07-03, qo13 C8 re-run): seeding kiro-auth-token.json with {accessToken, profileArn, expiresAt} ONLY — no refreshToken — is sufficient for standalone KAS 2.11.0 (a full spec-mode session ran on the bare access token). This removes the refresh-token-family risk from the original workaround: a cyril auth fix can materialize a refresh-less token file per spawn and never endanger the CLI's single-use refresh token. Also re-confirmed the trap: KAS self-refreshes the DEAD GitHub-social identity in the file (expiresAt was bumped during a failed run minutes before), so a fresh-looking expiresAt is NOT a validity signal — identity must be checked (authMethod/provider fields), or auth sourced from the CLI sqlite store directly.

AUTH ARCHITECTURE CONFIRMED (2026-07-03, live process table): CLI-launched KAS (acp --agent-engine v3) runs with --auth=acp-callback — auth via the _kiro/auth/getAccessToken callback from the CLI's sqlite store; no token file involved. The 2.5.0-era kiro-auth-token-cli.json sync contract is dead (file is a Jun 9 relic). So the default-SSO-file path this issue describes is hit ONLY by standalone spawns, and NO user action (re-login, opening KAS sessions) ever bridges sqlite -> that file. Fix direction sharpened: cyril should spawn acp-server.js with --auth=acp-callback and answer the callback from the sqlite store (profileArn from the state table), OR synthesize a refresh-less private token file and pass --token-path — never depend on the shared default file.

PROBE COMPLETE (2026-07-03, prove-it-prototype, branch feat/dcc6-kas-discovery-auth, artifacts .cyril-dcc6/): BOTH fix halves proven live against KAS 2.11.0. Probe A (versioned-dir glob + exact-version-match + newest/legacy fallback) chose the identical acp-server.js path kiro-cli itself spawns (oracle: /proc/<child>/cmdline of kiro-cli's own KAS spawn — byte-equal). Probe B (--auth=acp-callback + responder answering _kiro/auth/getAccessToken from sqlite auth_kv token + state-table profileArn) completed authenticated turns twice (backend = auth ground truth). KEY DISCOVERY via a broken-oracle detour: kiro-cli WRAPPER mode FORWARDS getAccessToken to its outer ACP client — an unanswered callback kills the turn with TokenInvalidError('Host refresh callback returned no access token'). So ONE sqlite-backed responder fixes both spawn modes, and cyril's current file-reading responder is the wrapper-mode failure cause. Expiry behavior NOT probed (all runs >=10min pre-expiry) — stale-token responder policy stays with cyril-taba. Next: falsifiable-design (open q: version-match tiebreak when no dir matches CLI version).

DESIGN READY (2026-07-04, falsifiable-design): .cyril-dcc6/design.md. Core: (A) discovery.rs selection policy exact-CLI-version > newest-semver > legacy > KasMissing::Server, dir grammar <semver>-<sha64> with inner-entry check, free-path argv gains --auth=acp-callback; (B) auth.rs source swap read_token_file -> read_sqlite_store (rusqlite bundled, optional, kas-gated, READ_ONLY, spawn_blocking), login gate = auth_kv row presence (logout deletes row). 14 claims w/ per-claim fences; cheapest falsifier 7/7 PASS (falsifier-selection-policy.py, incl. real-machine /proc cross-check); C8 fence fails against today's code by construction. Removed-invariant sweep: free-path now DEPENDS on responder dispatch (C14), file gate deleted (C10), sqlite newly opened (C12). Deferrals: taba (stale policy, reframed), lwpm (Windows store, filed), 0pms, l7tw, 6iek. Next: budgeted-plan after design approval.

PLAN READY (2026-07-04, budgeted-plan): .cyril-dcc6/plan.md — 7 slices, expand/contract (workspace green both feature sets after every slice): 1 pure select_server policy (C1-C4 fences incl. semver-vs-lex + partial-extraction) -> 2 wire into resolve w/ legacy fallback + override bypass + real CLI version (C5-C7) -> 3 rusqlite dep plumbing kas-gated (dep-tree oracle) -> 4 read_sqlite_store w/ snake_case + keyed-row + readonly-never-creates fences (C9/C11/C12) -> 5 responder swap + per-call re-read (C10/C13, logout/re-login mid-session fixtures) -> 6 argv flip --auth=acp-callback + sqlite login gate (C8/C14a; argv fence fails pre-slice by construction) -> 7 contract: delete SSO-file path, grep-zero fixture, smoke/doc alignment. KEY ORDERING: responder sqlite-backed BEFORE argv flip. Post-slice: one-time user-gated live smoke run (C14b), deterministic fences are the CI-permanent form. Next: checkpointed-build.

BUILD COMPLETE (2026-07-04, checkpointed-build, feat/dcc6-kas-discovery-auth): 6 commits (2768521 slice1 selection policy, 2d94448 slice2 wiring, 5395107 slice3 rusqlite dep, ff42b5d slices4+5 sqlite responder, 54df9e9 slice6 argv+gate, 0c59c1a slice7 contract). All fences green: C1-C7 selection/fallback/override, C8 argv byte-equal to kiro-cli's /proc-captured spawn, C9-C13 sqlite source (keyed rows, snake_case, readonly-never-creates, logout/re-login/corrupt), C14a gate_is_sqlite_not_file, grep 'kiro-auth-token' = 0 hits (resurrection tripwire). Workspace green both feature sets (default + kas), clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean, python falsifier 7/7 every slice. Plan deviations (all no-dead-code driven): slice1 wired select_server inertly; slices 4+5 merged; contract deletions landed incrementally (4+5: read_token_file; 6: TOKEN_FILE_REL). Dep deviation: rusqlite 0.39 not 0.40 (0.40's libsqlite3-sys needs unstable cfg_select, breaks pinned 1.94.0). REMAINING: C14b one-time live validation (user-gated, fresh token window): cargo test -p cyril-core --features kas --test kas_freepath_smoke --test kas_wrapper_smoke -- --ignored --nocapture.

C14b LIVE COMPLETE (2026-07-04 04:07Z, fresh token window): both hardened smokes PASS with the KAS_SMOKE_OK sentinel echoed through cyril's real bridge — free path (--auth=acp-callback + sqlite responder, 3.48s) AND wrapper (forwarded callback, 3.50s). Smokes hardened first: TurnCompleted-only was l7tw-vulnerable (an error turn collapses into TurnCompleted), and contains('ok') would false-green on TokenInvalidError ('ok' in 'token'). ALL design claims C1-C14 now verified incl. live. Branch feat/dcc6-kas-discovery-auth ready to merge.

PR OPEN (2026-07-04): https://github.com/dwalleck/cyril/pull/39. CI surfaced two gaps: (1) rustfmt diffs in the smoke prompts (python-scripted edits bypassed the format hook AND the local check read the pipe's exit, not rustfmt's — check exit codes directly); (2) commitlint (PR-only, first enforcement on this style) rejects probe/design/plan types and scopes with spaces — branch history REWRITTEN to conforming messages and force-pushed; COMMIT HASHES IN EARLIER NOTES ARE PRE-REWRITE (slice subjects now 'feat(cyril-dcc6): slice N - ...', probe->chore, design/plan->docs). Residual commitlint failure: the inherited main commit 'probe(cyril-qo13): ...' stays in the PR range until main is pushed.

Closed: Shipped: PR #39 merged to main (f8987e2, 2026-07-04). Versioned-dir KAS discovery (exact-CLI-version > newest-semver > legacy) + sqlite-backed --auth=acp-callback responder; all C1-C14 fences green incl. C14b live smokes (free path 3.48s, wrapper 3.50s, KAS_SMOKE_OK sentinel). Deferred: cyril-taba (stale-token policy), cyril-lwpm (Windows store), cyril-l7tw (error visibility).

title: Consolidate center-scroll window helpers and test buffer-flattening duplicated across widgets
description: Addendum (PR #58 gemini review, 7 comments, all one finding — verified valid): the shared buffer-flattening helper should use direct indexing buffer[(x,y)].symbol() rather than defensive .cell().map_or(...) — in-bounds is guaranteed by fixture geometry and direct indexing is the existing house test idiom (input.rs buffer_rows). Deferred here rather than patched 7 times pre-merge.

title: KAS-2a follow-up: turn_end vs prompt-response stop_reason fidelity on KAS cancel (first-to-arrive wins)
description:
Under KAS a turn produces TWO TurnCompleted: the agent's session_info_update -> turn_end (stop_reason from _meta.kiro.stopReason, scoped to the session) and the off-loop prompt-response synthesis (bridge.rs:563-574, sent as RoutedNotification::global). The bridge dedup (bridge.rs:1385) forwards whichever arrives FIRST and drops the second. For a normal turn both are EndTurn, so it is moot. On a USER CANCEL the prompt resolves Cancelled while turn_end's stopReason may differ; if turn_end wins the race carrying stopReason 'end_turn', the App records EndTurn instead of Cancelled — nondeterministic, dependent on KAS wire behavior on cancel (currently UNOBSERVED; no cancel turn_end has been captured).

Distinct from cyril-a71q: that residual is about clearing the WRONG turn; a per-turn id does NOT decide which of two same-turn signals carries the authoritative stop_reason. The design comment (bridge.rs:560-562) explicitly accepts 'either order' for the common (EndTurn) case.

ACTION before deciding a fix: capture a live KAS cancel (experiments/conductor-spike probe) — does turn_end fire on cancel? with what stopReason? If KAS already reports 'cancelled' in turn_end, there is no bug. If it reports 'end_turn' (or omits turn_end and only the prompt resolves Cancelled), decide whether the off-loop Cancelled must take precedence. Surfaced by /code-review max finders 2026-06-29. LOW severity: only manifests if KAS misreports the cancel stopReason in turn_end AND that frame wins the race.

title: KAS _kiro/frontendToolCall: model agent->client frontend-tool callback, reply declining (KAS-2)
description: New in kiro-cli 2.13.0 (KAS @kiro/agent 0.18.2 / observed in v2 TUI client): KAS can ask its client to execute a client-hosted ('frontend') tool via ext request _kiro/frontendToolCall. Request carries a ToolCallUpdate-like payload ({toolCallId, title, rawInput?, ...}); the client advertises the capability as frontendToolCall:true in KiroClientMeta handshake caps. The v2 TUI (2.13.0) registers a handler that declines every call with {outcome:"cancelled"} (log: 'declining unhosted client tool'). In relayed/cloud sessions it is one of three consent-callback kinds (permission/frontendToolCall/openUrl) with disconnect policy fail (permission defers 5min). Cyril's KAS dialect (ROADMAP KAS-2) should model the method and reply the same {outcome:"cancelled"} decline until cyril hosts client tools — an unhandled ext request risks hanging a KAS turn. Reference: docs/kiro-2.13.0-wire-audit.md.

title: KAS-7 — Advertise _meta.kiro.hooks at initialize + hooks-host responders; decide the v2 standalone-loader flag
description:
Implementation ticket for ROADMAP KAS-7 (hooks host — the org write/exec-policy interception point; fully de-risked, fired end-to-end 2026-06-16 via probe-kas-hooks-host-2.7.1.py). Core work per the milestone: advertise clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.hooks at initialize (top-level KiroClientMeta key, sibling of settings — NOT an AgentSettings key; composes with cyril-nhzw's handshake plumbing); implement _kiro/hooks/list + _kiro/hooks/executeHook responders (cyril owns the hook registry from the user's .kiro/hooks/*.json and spawns runCommand hooks: USER_PROMPT env, timeout default 60s, combined output, real exitCode, abort handle for _kiro/hooks/cancel); handle _kiro/hooks/{cancel,didChange}; preToolUse executeHook exitCode 2 blocks the tool with the output as reason. Shares the host-executor layer with KAS-5 fs/terminal callbacks.

NEW since the milestone was written (wire-verified 2026-07-17, probe-kas-hook-confirm-2.13.0.py, cyril-497j): the ROADMAP's 'no v2' note is STALE — 2.13.0 KAS reads an optional v2:true key in the same hooks object. v2 activates a SECOND, agent-side hook model: KAS's own standalone .kiro/hooks/*.json loader (kasHookFileSchema, file-watched, workspace + global roots), Stop-hook confirm dialogs (_meta.kiro.hookConfirm -> requestPermission), and the CreateHookTool. With {enabled:true, v2:true} the live capture drove ZERO _kiro/hooks/* host callbacks — everything ran agent-side, i.e. v2 shifts hook ownership from cyril to KAS.

DECISION REQUIRED before implementation: which model(s) cyril enables. enabled-only = cyril as executor/audit-gate (the strategic org-policy stage; hooks cyril controls); +v2 = users' on-disk KAS hooks light up with confirm dialogs for free (cyril-497j renders them) but execution moves agent-side, outside cyril's gate. They may compose (enabled governs host callbacks, v2 the standalone loader) — probe the enabled-only and enabled+v2 combinations A/B to confirm whether both paths can coexist before choosing a default. Document the choice in the KAS wire audit + covenant doc.


--- COUPLING NOTE 2026-07-18 (from cyril-0wyn triage) ---

KAS injects its hooks system-prompt briefing (hooksBlock: createHook tool, trigger
vocabulary, exit-code semantics incl. exit-2 block) ONLY when clientInfo.name maps to
'kiro-ide' (client === "kiro-ide" ? hooksBlock : ""). The hook MACHINERY this issue
implements (_meta.kiro.hooks advertisement + host responders) is gated separately, on
the handshake. Consequence: KAS-7 cannot be designed independently of the cyril-0wyn
name decision — name='kiro-cli' means hooks run against an unbriefed model (exit-2
blocks unexplained, hook-injected context unattributed); name=cyril -> kiro-ide
fallback means the model is briefed but with IDE-only UI pointers wrong for a TUI.
Mitigation candidate either way: cyril injects its own corrected hooks briefing via
steering/context whenever hooks are configured. See cyril-0wyn TRIAGE 2026-07-18 for
the verified hooksBlock contents.
acceptance_criteria: cyril sends _meta.kiro.hooks per the decided config on the KAS engine; _kiro/hooks/list+executeHook responders implemented with cancel/didChange handling and a preToolUse-block regression test; A/B probe capture recording which callbacks fire under enabled-only vs enabled+v2; ROADMAP/audit docs updated with the decided default.

title: KAS-5 — Host I/O callback responders: fs and terminal (first real cyril-stages interception point)
description: ROADMAP KAS-5 (docs/ROADMAP.md). Depends on KAS-2a (cyril-j16p) — the walking skeleton must render a turn first. Pulled ahead of KAS-3/KAS-4: the first cyril-stages deliverable, converging the KAS track with Phase 2 (ADR-0003). KAS delegates file I/O and shell execution to the host via server->client ACP requests; cyril becomes the executor and therefore the audit/gate/transform point. Scope: implement the HostIo capability sub-trait (the accessor stub pattern is laid down in KAS-0/cyril-atjw) + responders for fs read/write and terminal exec, routed through the single-mediator bridge loop (ADR-0004). INVARIANT INHERITED FROM KAS-0/ADR-0004: the loop forwards requests and NEVER awaits their resolution; cyril-side resolution that is slow (file read / shell exec) spawns OFF-LOOP, the same way the turn prompt does (cyril-84ca). Reintroducing a blocking await in the loop is a regression. Windows/WSL path translation applies at the fs boundary. Filed during KAS-0 budgeted-plan to cite the request-resolution deferral.

title: Bridge must reap KAS/kiro-cli children — orphaned acp-server.js processes accumulate
description: Observed 2026-07-03: 17 orphaned acp-server.js node servers (1-4 days old, reparented to systemd --user) left behind by dead kiro-cli/probe/test parents — kiro-cli does NOT kill its KAS child on exit, and stdio-transport servers with a dead pipe partner idle forever (~GBs of RAM). Upstream bug in kiro-cli, but cyril must defend: when cyril's bridge (or kas_*_smoke harnesses / probe scripts) spawns kiro-cli or acp-server.js directly, ensure the whole child process group dies with the parent (kill_on_drop + process-group kill on Linux; probes should terminate() in finally). Cleanup performed this session: killed all 17 (parent check: PPID == systemd --user).
acceptance_criteria: cyril exit (clean or panic) leaves no acp-server.js or kiro-cli descendants; probe/smoke harnesses kill children in teardown; a repeated spawn/kill cycle shows zero process accumulation.
notes: Closed: Fixed: PR #44 (108429a + 9ed33db portable fence probe). Repro: dropped_agent_process_kills_process_group (child + grandchild die on drop via process_group(0) + nix killpg guard; kill_on_drop backstop). Harnesses covered by inheritance; 62 probe scripts already had teardown.

title: Watch: KAS 0.8.0 new surface _kiro/knowledge/indexing{Started,Completed} + _kiro/sandbox/applyConfig

A knowledge progress panel styled like crew_panel (bordered block, cyan ` knowledge ` title, `{icon} ` + bold-white `{name:<20}` + status, `+N more` overflow). title: KAS-2a follow-up: turn-seq dedup so a stale TurnCompleted can't clear a later same-session turn
description:
Slice 3 of cyril-j16p dedupes the double TurnCompleted (turn_end notification + prompt response) with a 'drop when turn_in_flight is None' guard in the bridge loop observer (bridge.rs:1370). Correct for the common case but has a residual: turn A's late prompt-response TurnCompleted, arriving AFTER turn_end cleared the flag AND a new same-session turn B started, would match B's turn_in_flight (session id is not per-turn unique) and wrongly clear it. Fix: give TurnCompleted a monotonic per-turn id (bridge.rs:562 already anticipates 'a turn id field to TurnCompleted'); the observer clears/forwards only on an id match. Low-risk for the KAS-2a walking skeleton (narrow window, trivial turns); deferred from cyril-j16p Slice 3. Ref ROADMAP:KAS-2a.

CROSS-SESSION DIMENSION (independently surfaced by all 5 /code-review max finders, 2026-06-29 — strong observability signal): the same turn_in_flight: Option<SessionId> gate is also session-blind. The observer (bridge.rs:1385) matches TurnCompleted by variant only and never compares routed.session_id. A non-in-flight session's turn_end -> TurnCompleted (e.g. a future KAS spec/secondary/parallel-turn session, per docs/kiro-2.7.1-wire-audit) would clear the MAIN turn's gate (premature un-busy + early SendPrompt accept), or be dropped when the gate is None. NOT reachable in KAS-2a (single session; KAS subagents are tool_calls with no per-session turn_end). The monotonic per-turn-id fix above ALSO resolves this, provided the id is globally unique across (session, turn). CONSTRAINT for the fixer: the off-loop prompt-response TurnCompleted is sent as RoutedNotification::global (session_id None, event.rs:251), so a naive 'clear only when routed.session_id == turn_in_flight' fix WITHOUT a turn id would break v2 — the global signal never matches Some(main), freezing every v2 turn busy. The fix must key on a turn id, not a bare session id.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) HOLE in the turn-id fix as sketched: the bridge loop can stamp only the SYNTHESIZED prompt-response TurnCompleted; the KAS turn_end TurnCompleted is produced by the converter (convert/kas.rs:34) from a wire frame the bridge cannot stamp — a stale same-session turn_end still matches by session, so the residual survives for that signal. Either (a) probe whether session_info_update carries a native turn identifier, or (b) the observer tracks 'one duplicate still expected for the previous (session, seq)'. The variant likely needs Option<TurnId> with dual matching (id-match for synthesized, session-match for wire-scoped turn_end) — a legitimate Option-for-absent, not a sentinel. Make it a TurnId(u64) newtype per CLAUDE.md (mirror TerminalRegistry's saturating_add counter idiom, terminal_io.rs:106).
(2) Design JOINTLY with cyril-pnwb — same observer code (bridge.rs:1370-1393); turn identity and stop-reason precedence are one 'which signal is authoritative' question, not two.
(3) Cross-session nuance to fold in: a non-in-flight session's turn_end clears the bridge gate while the App routes the scoped TurnCompleted to a subagent stream — a SPLIT-BRAIN (bridge accepts a new prompt, main UI stays Busy) rather than the premature un-busy described. Same fix covers it.

title: KAS — Marshal kiro-cli settings into the _meta.kiro.settings (AgentSettings) handshake
description:
On KAS (v3), per-session feature flags ride the wire as AgentSettings in the initialize handshake: clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.settings (KiroClientMeta; covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md section 3, lines 98-104). cyril sends only {cwd, mcpServers} with NO _meta.kiro.settings, so on KAS EVERY feature flag is off: toolSearch (MCP token savings), thinking, knowledge, codeIntelligence, subagentOrchestration, compaction, todoList, checkpoint, semanticReview, fta, tangentMode, disableAutoCompaction, and the _c2s/_subagent/_delegate/_providerPowers/_parallelTasks/etc. family. KAS thus runs in a degraded all-flags-off mode.

The v2 TUI assembles this object from the kiro-cli settings file (function zme() in tui.js) and hands it to the ACP client constructor. cyril has no equivalent and no AgentSettings type (called out as a gap in covenant section 10).

CONTRAST WITH v2 (why this is KAS-only): the v2 Rust backend reads toolSearch (and other settings) DIRECTLY from the kiro-cli settings file across the ACP boundary — free for cyril, same pattern as trust-persistence. KAS is a separate Node process that cannot read the Rust settings store, so the client must marshal settings over the wire. See memory reference_kiro_tool_search_setting.

SCOPE: read kiro-cli settings (global + workspace) and build _meta.kiro.settings matching the AgentSettings shape, sent in the KAS initialize handshake. Default-sensitive flags (covenant section 3): semanticReview default ON if absent; fta default OFF; subagentOrchestration true -> orchestrate_subagent tool, false/absent -> invoke_sub_agent (CHANGES the subagent tool — directly affects KAS-3 rendering); toolSearch: {enabled, minPct, minTokens, neverDefer}; knowledge/compaction nested objects.

WHY IT MATTERS: gates MCP token savings via toolSearch (governs the tools-bucket size in cyril-5et2's context bar), thinking, and subagent-engine selection (KAS-3). Foundational enabler shared by multiple downstream KAS milestones.

DEPENDS ON: KAS-1 auth responder (KAS sessions must actually run first; not yet filed as its own issue) and the KAS-0 engine gate (cyril-atjw). This is the SETTINGS half of the same initialize._meta.kiro handshake whose AUTH half is KAS-1.

ROADMAP NOTE: the ROADMAP currently spells out only the auth half of the KAS-1 handshake; AgentSettings appears only piecemeal (subagentOrchestration at docs/ROADMAP.md:209). Recommend making AgentSettings explicit under KAS-1 (doc edit, separate from this ticket).

REFS: covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md section 3; memory reference_kiro_tool_search_setting; v2 TUI zme().
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine, cyril sends clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.settings in initialize, populated from kiro-cli settings (global + workspace) and matching the AgentSettings shape.
* toolSearch.{enabled,minPct,minTokens} is forwarded and observably takes effect on KAS (tool_search advertised, MCP tools deferred out of the loaded set).
* Default-sensitive flags handled correctly: semanticReview default-ON-if-absent, fta default-OFF, subagentOrchestration selects orchestrate_subagent vs invoke_sub_agent.
* A new AgentSettings type in cyril-core with serde camelCase, unit-tested for wire shape + the default-sensitive flags.
* v1/v2 engine path is unchanged (no _meta.kiro.settings sent or required there).
notes: Closed: Shipped: PR #37 (settings handshake, merged 2026-07-01) + PR #38 ($KIRO_HOME + zme knowledge block, code-review follow-up). A/B probe f08ba87 confirms KAS honors _meta.kiro.settings via the subagentOrchestration oracle (orchestrate_subagent vs invoke_sub_agent). CAVEAT: the toolSearch AC (tool_search advertised + MCP tools deferred) was NOT the probed flag, and tool_search availability has a backend axis (2.13.0 finding) — if toolSearch misbehaves on KAS, probe that flag specifically before reopening. Workspace-overlay slice split to cyril-sa39.

title: KAS session-listing: model _kiro/session/list response-shape change (source/executionTarget/status/warnings)
description:
KAS 0.17.2 (kiro-cli 2.12.3) changed _kiro/session/list on the wire. cyril does NOT consume session/list today (v2 has empty sessionCapabilities; KAS session-listing is future KAS work) — this is a MODELING task for when cyril adds KAS session management, not an urgent parser fix.

REQUEST (additive, gated — safe): dispatch fields ride on _meta.kiro, all optional-with-defaults: sessionSource ('local'|'remote'|'all', default local), listScope ('workspace'|'user', default workspace), executionTarget ({kind:'local'|'cloud-sandbox'}). Non-default listScope/remote source -> InvalidParamsError unless the agent is remoteConfigured (KIRO_REMOTE_SESSIONS_ENDPOINT set). A client sending nothing gets exactly the old behavior.

RESPONSE (CHANGED shape): 2.12.1 returned bare summaries (listSessions({})). 2.12.3 returns { sessions: [ { sessionId, cwd, title, updatedAt, _meta: { kiro: { agentMode, createdAt, source:'local'|'remote', executionTarget:{kind}, status (active-aware, default 'idle'), parentSessionId?, description? } } } ], _meta?: { kiro: { warnings } } }. buildSessionInfoRow folds source+executionTarget+status per entry; top-level _meta.kiro.warnings aggregates partial-failure notices (e.g. remote-list timeout under Promise.allSettled).

When cyril models KAS session-listing, cover: source tag, executionTarget placement (local vs relayed cloud-sandbox), active-aware status, parent linkage, and the warnings channel. Ref: docs/kiro-2.12.3-wire-audit.md; per feedback_model_full_wire_surface, model all fields or explicit-ignore + debug-log unknowns. Sibling of cyril-tikf (handshake caps that gate this).

description:
KAS-8 functional gap. `_kiro/error/rate_limit` — an agent->client notification KAS emits when the backend rate-limits a turn. cyril currently drops it via the KAS-2a unknown-variant debug! arm, so the user sees a stalled/empty turn with no explanation.

SCOPE:
- Add a converter arm for `_kiro/error/rate_limit`.
- Surface it as a system message (rate limited — explain + when to retry if the payload carries it); clear on the next successful turn.
- Make sure it interacts correctly with the KAS turn-end / busy-clear path (a rate-limited turn must release the busy guard — see cyril-j16p non-blocking criterion).

notes: Closed: Shipped: PR #59 merged to main (9737f1e). KAS _kiro/error/rate_limit now routes to RateLimited via merged match arm. All 6 AC fences pass: converter (3), engine (1), busy-preservation (1), unknown-drop (1). Fixed in-branch: dead probe file removed per bot review. Filed: none (no new issues discovered).

title: KAS host-io: atomic write_text_file (temp + rename) to avoid corrupting files on partial write
description:
kas::host_io::write_text_file does tokio::fs::write (truncate + write) directly at the target path. A crash, disk-full, or error mid-write leaves a truncated/corrupt file in the user's working tree with the original content gone.

Since cyril is now the executor of the agent's writes (ADR-0003), durable writes belong at this layer: write to a temp file in the SAME directory, fsync, then atomic rename over the target -- matching the project's session-sidecar MOVED_TO cadence and the rust-best-practices atomic-write guidance. Decide behavior for symlink targets and permission/ownership preservation. Keep the existing mkdir -p (create_dir_all) for missing parents. Discovered during review of PR #34 (KAS-5a).
acceptance_criteria: An interrupted write never leaves a partial file at the target (original preserved, or fully-new content -- never half); temp + rename happens within the target's directory; empty-content and parent-dir-creation tests still pass; symlink-target behavior documented.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — PROMOTED P3->P2: since ADR-0003 makes cyril the executor of every KAS agent write, this is the only open issue whose failure mode is silent user-data loss (bare tokio::fs::write truncate-in-place, host_io.rs:52). Land before KAS becomes a daily driver.

Two footguns the plan must solve concretely (not just 'decide behavior'):
(1) Permission clobbering — NamedTempFile creates mode 0600 and persist() silently downgrades an existing 0644/0755 target. fs::metadata(target) + set_permissions on the temp BEFORE rename; umask-respecting mode for fresh files.
(2) Symlink write-through — rename replaces a symlink with a regular file (today's tokio::fs::write writes THROUGH it). canonicalize before choosing temp dir + rename target.

Implementation shape: whole sequence in ONE tokio::task::spawn_blocking with sync tempfile::NamedTempFile::new_in(parent) + write_all + sync_all + persist — single threadpool hop, temp auto-cleans on failure via Drop. Update the host_io.rs:9-18 doc comment (spawn_blocking is the sanctioned sync form). Dir-fsync optional: its failure mode is 'old content survives', which the AC permits; if added, unix-gate it (dir sync_all fails on Windows). Housekeeping: tempfile is currently dev-only in cyril-core (Cargo.toml) — promote via { workspace = true }.

Closed: Shipped: PR #49 merged to main (36b9272). All 6 ACs fenced: atomicity (write_atomic_unwritable_parent_errs_target_intact + SIGKILL oracle), temp-in-target-dir (message fence + TMPDIR oracle S18), empty/parents tests unedited, symlink behavior documented+fenced (write-through preserved, dangling refused), permission clobbering solved (mode preserved incl. 0600 anti-widening; fresh files umask-parity via File::create control oracle), tempfile promoted as kas-optional dep (zero default-graph edges). Live kas_fs_host_io_smoke passed against kiro-cli 2.11.0. Behavior changes (user-approved D1-D3): read-only targets refused, dangling symlinks error, unwritable-parent has no in-place fallback. Fixed in-branch: 7 pre-PR review findings (F1-F7, .cyril-0v42/review-decisions.md). Filed: none.

title: Probe kiro-cli XDG_DATA_HOME handling — cyril hardcodes ~/.local/share/kiro-cli (KAS root + credential store)
description: PR-39 review finding (failure-hunter #10, code-reviewer S5): discovery.rs derives both the KAS extraction root and data.sqlite3 from the hardcoded KIRO_DATA_DIR_REL='.local/share/kiro-cli'. The 2.11.0 kiro-cli binary contains the XDG_DATA_HOME string (3 hits — dirs-crate pattern), so it plausibly honors it; if a user sets XDG_DATA_HOME, kiro-cli would extract/login elsewhere and cyril would emit loud-but-WRONG 'not found'/'not servable' errors while kiro-cli itself works. Probe: XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/x kiro-cli login + self-extract, check where data.sqlite3 and kas/<ver>-<sha>/ land. If honored: read the env var (absolute paths only) with ~/.local/share fallback — one-line change now that KIRO_DATA_DIR_REL is the single spelling. Related: cyril-lwpm (Windows-host store path).

title: K1b — Queue-steering TUI UX
description: User-facing steering UX on top of K1a plumbing. Enter-while-busy: when session.status()==Busy and input is non-slash text, route submit to SteerSession instead of session/prompt (idle unchanged; also fixes the latent second-prompt-mid-turn bug). Local echo in the transcript (steer: <msg> — queued), updated when steering_consumed arrives. Toolbar chip while >=1 steer queued, cleared on consumed/cleared/turn-end. /steer command for the idle path. Advisory UI copy (the agent weighs the steer and can decline).

title: clientInfo presentAs escape hatch: opt-in 'kiro-cli' impersonation for the memoryEnabled remote-tools branch
description:
DEFERRED FROM the cyril-0wyn ADR (clientInfo.name decision).
